definition_zh: v  取消， 废除； 抵消， 对消； 删去， 划掉
definition_en: to decide that something that was officially planned will not happen
example: Our flight was cancelled.

definition_zh: adj  爆炸的， 爆发的； 使人冲动的; n 爆炸物，炸药
definition_en: able or likely to explode; a substance that can cause an explosion
example: Because the gas is highly explosive, it needs to be kept in high-pressure containers.

definition_zh: v  准备， 预备
definition_en: to make plans or arrangements for something that will happen in the future
example: The prosecution wanted more time to prepare their case.

definition_zh: v  加入十字军， 投身正义运动; n 十字军；斗争，运动
definition_en: to take part in a crusade; a determined attempt to change something because you think you are morally right

definition_zh: n  小村庄
definition_en: a very small village

definition_zh: n  外科医生
definition_en: a doctor who does operations in a hospital
example: ...a heart surgeon.

definition_zh: n  门厅； 礼堂
definition_en: the area just inside the door of a house or other building, that leads to other rooms
example: a huge tiled entrance hall

definition_zh: vt  使泄气， 使灰心； 阻止， 劝阻
definition_en: to make someone less confident or less willing to do something
example: You should not let one failure discourage you.

definition_zh: v  事先影响某人； 偏爱； 易受感染
example: They take pains to hire people whose personalities predispose them to serve customers well.

definition_zh: vt  监督， 监视； 俯瞰， 眺望
definition_en: to be in charge of a group of workers and check that a piece of work is done satisfactorily
example: A team leader was appointed to oversee the project.

definition_zh: n  污染， 污染物； 玷污
definition_en: the process of making air, water, soil etc dangerously dirty and not suitable for people to use, or the state of being dangerously dirty
example: California’s tough anti-pollution laws

definition_zh: v  装作， 假装
definition_en: to behave as if something is true when in fact you know it is not, in order to deceive people or for fun
example: Let’s pretend we’re on the moon.

definition_zh: n  传送； 对流
definition_en: the movement in a gas or liquid caused by warm gas or liquid rising, and cold gas or liquid sinking
example: ...clouds which lift warm, moist air by convection high into the atmosphere.

definition_zh: v 保证；
definition_en: to promise that something is true
example: Therefore, it is important to note that individual situations may warrant including additional characters in the list of special characters.

definition_zh: n 【天】
definition_en: the path along which the Sun seems to move
example: On the astrolabe, an off - centered circle represents the orbit of the sun - the ecliptic.

definition_zh: vt  隐藏； 隐瞒； 掩盖
definition_en: to hide something carefully
example: The shadows concealed her as she crept up to the house.

definition_zh: vt  对…评价过高， 高估
definition_en: If you say that something or someone is overrated, you mean that people have a higher opinion of them than they deserve
example: More men are finding out that the joys of work have been overrated.

definition_zh: adv  与此同时
definition_en: while something else is happening
example: Cook the sauce over a medium heat until it thickens. Meanwhile start boiling the water for the pasta.

definition_zh: adj  豪华的， 华丽的， 富裕的； 丰富的， 丰饶的
definition_en: very beautiful, with a lot of decoration, and made from expensive materials
example: evening dresses in opulent fabrics

definition_zh: vi  同时发生； 一致
definition_en: to happen at the same time as something else, especially by chance
example: When our vacations coincided, we often holidayed together.

definition_zh: adj  有能力的； 能胜任的
definition_en: having enough skill or knowledge to do something to a satisfactory standard
example: A competent mechanic should be able to fix the problem.

definition_zh: vt  需要； 命令； 规定， 要求
definition_en: to need something
example: Campbell’s broken leg will probably require surgery.

definition_zh: n 划伤，
definition_en: a small cut on someone’s skin
example: There were deep scratches all over her face.

definition_zh: adj  有同情心的， 表示怜悯的
definition_en: feeling sympathy for people who are suffering
example: a caring, compassionate man

definition_zh: adj 合作的，协作的; n  合作社
definition_en: willing to cooperate; a business or organization owned equally by all the people working there
example: He was doing his best to be cooperative.

definition_zh: n  战略家
definition_en: someone who is good at planning things, especially military or political actions
example: Military strategists had devised a plan that guaranteed a series of stunning victories.

definition_zh: adj  急躁的， 易怒的， 易受刺激的； 过敏的
definition_en: getting annoyed quickly or easily
example: Jo was tired, irritable, and depressed.

definition_zh: adj 三部分的；三倍的; v  增至三倍
definition_en: having three parts or involving three groups, people, events etc; to increase by three times as much, or to make something do this
example: a triple murder investigation

definition_zh: adj  说话不多的， 内向的； 有所保留的， 预订的
definition_en: A table in a restaurant or a seat in a theatre that is reserved is being kept for someone rather than given or sold to anyone else
example: Seats, or sometimes entire tables, were reserved.

definition_zh: n 情绪; vt  使缓和
definition_en: the way you are feeling at a particular time, especially when you are feeling angry for a short time; to make something less severe or extreme
example: Pete hit his brother in a fit of temper .

definition_zh: v 发痒；使高兴，逗乐; n  痒
definition_en: if something touching your body tickles you, it makes you want to rub your body because it is slightly uncomfortable; a feeling in your throat that makes you want to cough
example: Mommy, this blanket tickles.

definition_zh: v  反对， 不赞成; n 物体；对象；目标；宾语[əb'dʒekt]
definition_en: to feel or say that you oppose or disapprove of something; a solid thing that you can hold, touch, or see but that is not alive
example: If no one objects, I would like Mrs Harrison to be present.

definition_zh: adj  能接近的； 易达到的
definition_en: a place, building, or object that is accessible is easy to reach or get into
example: The island is only accessible by boat.

definition_zh: v  悬挂， 吊， 垂下； 吊死， 绞死
definition_en: to kill someone by dropping them with a rope around their neck, or to die in this way, especially as a punishment for a serious crime
example: If he is found guilty, he will almost certainly hang.

definition_zh: vt  鉴别， 证明
definition_en: to prove that something is true or real
example: The painting has been authenticated by experts.

definition_zh: v  满足， 满意； 足够
definition_en: to be enough
example: A light lunch will suffice .

definition_zh: v  承认； 让步
definition_en: to admit that something is true or correct, although you wish it were not true
example: ‘That’s the only possible solution.’ ‘Yes, I suppose so,’ Charles conceded.

definition_zh: adj 值得注意的；显著的，杰出的，显赫的; n  名人， 要人
definition_en: important, interesting, excellent, or unusual enough to be noticed or mentioned
example: Every country in the world signed the treaty, with one notable exception - the United States.

definition_zh: adj  无双的， 无比的， 空前的
definition_en: bigger, better, or worse than anything else
example: an achievement unparalleled in sporting history

definition_zh: adj 豪华的，舒适的; n  长毛绒
definition_en: very comfortable, expensive, and of good quality; a silk or cotton material with a thick soft surface
example: a plush hotel

definition_zh: v  蒸发； 消失； 不复存在
definition_en: if a liquid evaporates, or if heat evaporates it, it changes into a gas
example: Most of the water had evaporated.

definition_zh: n 委托；佣金; vt  授权， 委托
definition_en: an extra amount of money that is paid to a person or organization according to the value of the goods they have sold or the services they have provided; to formally ask someone to write an official report, produce a work of art for you etc
example: The dealer takes a 20% commission on the sales he makes.

definition_zh: n  楼梯
definition_en: a set of stairs inside a building with its supports and the side parts that you hold on to
example: They walked down the staircase together.

definition_zh: v  轻抛； 轻弹
definition_en: to make a flat object such as a coin go upwards and turn over in the air
example: We flipped a coin to see who would go first.

definition_zh: v  连接， 衔接； 联合， 关联； 由…联想到； 给…接通电话
definition_en: to join two or more things together
example: We’d like two rooms with connecting doors (= doors that join the rooms ) .

definition_zh: v  重新开始， 继续恢复
definition_en: to start doing something again after stopping or being interrupted
example: She hopes to resume work after the baby is born.

definition_zh: vt 致函，写姓名地址；向…讲话，演说；处理，
definition_en: if you address an envelope, package etc, you write on it the name and address of the person you are sending it to
example: Send a stamped, self-addressed envelope (=  with your address on it so it can be sent back to you  ) .

definition_zh: n  姿势； 心情； 态度
definition_en: the way you position your body when sitting or standing
example: her upright posture

definition_zh: adj 螺旋形的，盘旋的; v 盘旋上升；急剧增长; n  螺旋形；逐渐加速上升
definition_en: Spiral is also an adjective; to move in a continuous curve that gets nearer to or further from its central point as it goes round; A spiral is a shape which winds around and around, with each curve above or outside the previous one
example: ...a spiral staircase.

definition_zh: vt  斩首
definition_en: to cut off someone’s head
example: a decapitated body

definition_zh: v 暂停；踌躇；
adj  过分的， 不合理的
definition_en: an exorbitant price, amount of money etc is much higher than it should be
example: Exorbitant housing prices have created an acute shortage of affordable housing for the poor.

definition_zh: n  外科学； 外科手术； 手术室， 诊疗室
definition_en: medical treatment in which a surgeon cuts open your body to repair or remove something inside
example: Dr. Hanson is in surgery.

definition_zh: adj  暴力的； 带有强烈感情的； 猛烈的
definition_en: involving actions that are intended to injure or kill people, by hitting them, shooting them etc
example: the increase in violent crime

definition_zh: v 解决，解答；决定，决意；分解，分析; n  决心， 决意
definition_en: to find a satisfactory way of dealing with a problem or difficulty; strong determination to succeed in doing something
example: Barnet was desperate for money to resolve his financial problems.

definition_zh: vt  遭遇； 面对， 正视
definition_en: if a problem, difficulty etc confronts you, it appears and needs to be dealt with
example: The problems confronting the new government were enormous.

definition_zh: vt  部署； 使用， 运用
definition_en: to organize or move soldiers, military equipment etc so that they are in the right place and ready to be used
example: a job in which a variety of professional skills will be deployed

definition_zh: v  繁殖， 生育； 复制， 仿造， 翻版； 再现， 使…在脑海中重现
definition_en: if an animal or plant reproduces, or reproduces itself, it produces young plants or animals
example: The turtles return to the coast to reproduce.

definition_zh: vi 聚集在一起; n  杂乱的一堆； 拥挤
definition_en: If people huddle in a group, they gather together to discuss something quietly or secretly; a group of people or things that are close together, but not arranged in any particular order, pattern, or system
example: Off to one side, Sticht, Macomber, Jordan, and Kreps huddled to discuss something.

definition_zh: n  表演， 扮演， 演唱； 翻译； 视觉再现
definition_en: someone’s performance of a play, piece of music etc
example: He gave a moving rendition of Lennon’s ‘Imagine’.

definition_zh: n  道路交叉口， 十字路口
definition_en: a place where roads, lines etc cross each other, especially where two roads meet
example: We crossed at a busy intersection.

definition_zh: n  奖学金； 学问， 学识
definition_en: an amount of money that is given to someone by an educational organization to help pay for their education
example: Her latest publication is a fine piece of scholarship.

definition_zh: adv 除此之外，而且
definition_en: used when adding another reason
example: I need the money. And besides, when I agree to do something, I do it.

definition_zh: vt  滴注； 逐渐灌输； 逐步培养
definition_en: to teach someone to think, behave, or feel in a particular way over a period of time
example: The tough thing is trying to instil a winning attitude in the kids.

definition_zh: adj  易燃的； 易怒的
definition_en: inflammable materials or substances will start to burn very easily
example: Petrol is highly inflammable.

definition_zh: vt 使生效，实施；贯彻，执行['ImplImənt]; n  工具
definition_en: to take action or make changes that you have officially decided should happen; a tool, especially one used for outdoor physical work
example: The government promised to implement a new system to control financial loan institutions.

definition_zh: v 出口；输出，传播['3kspɔːt]; n  出口； 输出； 出口商品
definition_en: to sell goods to another country; the business of selling and sending goods to other countries
example: Italian food has been exported all over the world.

definition_zh: n  安宁； 福利
vt  焚化， 焚毁， 把…烧成灰烬
definition_en: to burn something completely in order to destroy it
example: All the infected clothing was incinerated.

definition_zh: n  想象
definition_en: an exciting and unusual experience or situation you imagine happening to you, but which will probably never happen
example: I used to have fantasies about living in Paris with an artist.

definition_zh: v  侵蚀， 腐蚀； 削弱， 损害
definition_en: if the weather erodes rock or soil, or if rock or soil erodes, its surface is gradually destroyed
example: The cliffs are being constantly eroded by heavy seas.

definition_zh: v 检查，核对；阻止，
definition_en: to do something in order to find out whether something really is correct, true, or in good condition
example: Check the tiles carefully before you buy them.

definition_zh: vt  使沮丧； 挫败
definition_en: if something frustrates you, it makes you feel annoyed or angry because you are unable to do what you want
example: The fact that he’s working with amateurs really frustrates him.

definition_zh: adj  华丽的； 极好的
definition_en: extremely beautiful or attractive
example: ‘What do you think of my new flatmate?’ ‘He’s absolutely gorgeous !’

definition_zh: adj  务实的； 实事求是的； 实用主义观点的
definition_en: dealing with problems in a sensible practical way instead of strictly following a set of ideas
example: Williams took a more pragmatic approach to management problems.

definition_zh: n  甲烷， 沼气
definition_en: a gas that you cannot see or smell, which can be burned to give heat
example: Methane is one of the principal gases contributing to the greenhouse effect.

definition_zh: n  腐蚀； 磨损
definition_en: the process by which rock or soil is gradually destroyed by wind, rain, or the sea
example: the problem of soil erosion

definition_zh: v  攀登； 上升， 升高
definition_en: to move up through the air
example: The plane ascended rapidly.

definition_zh: adj  迷信的； 根据迷信的， 由迷信引起的； 受迷信思想支配的
definition_en: influenced by superstitions
example: a superstitious woman

definition_zh: vt  重新定方位； 重新定位； 使适应
conj  倘若， 只要， 假如
definition_en: used to say that something will only be possible if something else happens or is done
example: He can come with us, provided he pays for his own meals.

definition_zh: adj  必不可少的， 必需的
definition_en: someone or something that is indispensable is so important or useful that it is impossible to manage without them
example: Mobile phones have become an indispensable part of our lives.

definition_zh: n  透镜； 镜片； 镜头
definition_en: the part of a camera through which the light travels before it reaches the film
example: a standard 50mm lens

definition_zh: vt  表达， 体现； 具体表现， 使具体化； 包括， 包含
definition_en: to be a very good example of an idea or quality
example: She embodies everything I admire in a teacher.

definition_zh: vt  使适应， 使熟悉情况； 给…定位， 给…定向； 使朝向； 转至特定方向； 适应
adj  细菌的； 由细菌引起的
definition_en: Bacterial is used to describe things that relate to or are caused by bacteria
example: Cholera is a bacterial infection.

definition_zh: n  动机， 目的
definition_en: the reason that makes someone do something, especially when this reason is kept hidden
example: What do you suppose the killer’s motive was?

definition_zh: vt  展望， 想象； 面对
definition_en: to think that something is likely to happen in the future
example: The scheme cost a lot more than we had originally envisaged.

definition_zh: n  预料
definition_en: when you are expecting something to happen
example: She waited in eager anticipation for Robert to arrive.

definition_zh: vt  使有动机； 激励， 激发
definition_en: to make someone want to achieve something and make them willing to work hard in order to do this
example: A good teacher has to be able to motivate her students.

definition_zh: n  增援， 加强， 加固
definition_en: the act of making something stronger
example: The bridge needs some structural reinforcement.

definition_zh: n 大学导师的辅导课；
definition_en: a period of teaching and discussion with a tutor, especially in a British university
example: the tutorial system

definition_zh: v 损坏，破坏，糟蹋；宠坏，溺爱；变质
definition_en: to have a bad effect on something so that it is no longer attractive, enjoyable, useful etc
example: The whole park is spoiled by litter.

definition_zh: v  增加， 繁殖； 乘
definition_en: to breed
example: Bacteria multiply quickly in warm food.

definition_zh: n 牧场，草原; vt  放牧
definition_en: land or a field that is covered with grass and is used for cattle, sheep etc to feed on; to put animals outside in a field to feed on the grass
example: large areas of rough upland pasture

definition_zh: n  虚弱， 衰弱
definition_en: bad health or a particular illness
example: Health is a state of complete physical, mental and social well-being and not merely the absence of disease or infirmity.

definition_zh: n 飞行员，引航员; vt  驾驶， 为…引航
definition_en: someone who operates the controls of an aircraft or spacecraft; to guide an aircraft, spacecraft, or ship as its pilot
example: an airline pilot

definition_zh: n  赞成， 同意； 正式批准
definition_en: when a plan, decision, or person is officially accepted
example: The president has already given his approval to the plan.

definition_zh: adj  有疗效的； 医疗的
definition_en: able to or intended to cure illness
example: the spring’s alleged curative properties

definition_zh: adj 多重的；多样的; n  倍数
definition_en: many, or involving many things, people, events etc; a number that contains a smaller number an exact number of times
example: Baxter was rushed to the hospital with multiple stab wounds.

definition_zh: n  微生物学
definition_en: the scientific study of very small living things such as  bacteria
example: ...a professor of microbiology and immunology.

definition_zh: n  磁铁， 磁石； 磁体； 有吸引力的人或物
definition_en: a piece of iron or steel that can stick to metal or make other metal objects move towards itself
example: Prospect Park, with its vast lake, is a magnet for all health freaks.

definition_zh: vt  使暴露， 揭露
definition_en: to show something that is usually covered or hidden
example: He lifted his T-shirt to expose a jagged scar across his chest.

definition_zh: adj  多才多艺的， 多面手的； 多用途的， 通用的
definition_en: someone who is versatile has many different skills
example: a very versatile performer

definition_zh: v 全神贯注，全力以赴；集中，聚集；浓缩; n  浓缩物， 浓缩液
definition_en: to be present in large numbers or amounts somewhere, or to cause people or things to be present in large numbers or amounts somewhere; a substance or liquid which has been made stronger by removing most of the water from it
example: Construction of the aircraft is being concentrated at Prestwick.

definition_zh: prep  与…相对， 对抗
definition_en: used to show that two people or teams are competing against each other in a game or court case
example: the New York Knicks versus the LA Lakers

definition_zh: vt  居住于， 生活于
definition_en: if an area is populated by a particular group of people, they live there
example: The highlands are populated mainly by peasant farmers.

definition_zh: adj  直到最近的； 现代的
definition_en: If something is up-to-date, it is the newest thing of its kind
example: ...the most up-to-date information available on foods today.

definition_zh: adj  适当的， 相配的； 合适的， 适宜的
definition_en: having the right qualities for a particular person, purpose, or situation
example: We are hoping to find a suitable school.

definition_zh: n  全体人员， 员工
definition_en: the people who work in a company, organization, or military force
example: All personnel are to receive security badges.

definition_zh: v  溅； 泼
definition_en: if a liquid splashes, it hits or falls on something and makes a noise
example: The children were splashing about in the pool.

definition_zh: n  观点， 见解； 展望， 前景
definition_en: what is expected to happen in the future
example: Exercise will improve your looks and your outlook.

definition_zh: n  恋爱； 浪漫爱情； 爱情小说， 传奇故事
definition_en: love, or a feeling of being in love
example: The romance had gone out of their relationship.

definition_zh: n  喷泉
definition_en: a structure from which water is pushed up into the air, used for example as decoration in a garden or park
example: ...the fountains on the 16th Street Mall.

definition_zh: adj  不相关的； 离题的
definition_en: not useful or not relating to a particular situation, and therefore not important
example: We’re focussing too much on irrelevant details.

definition_zh: n  小巧的器械， 精巧的装置； 小玩意
definition_en: a small, useful, and cleverly-designed machine or tool
example: a neat gadget for sharpening knives

definition_zh: n  同胞； 同国人
definition_en: someone who was born in or is a citizen of the same country as someone else
example: Chris Robertson of Australia beat his compatriot Chris Dittmar in the final.

definition_zh: n  含意； 暗示， 暗指； 卷入， 牵连
definition_en: a situation in which it is shown or suggested that someone or something is involved in a crime or a dishonest act
example: They are called ‘Supertrams’, the implication being that (= which is meant to suggest that ) they are more advanced than earlier models.

definition_zh: adj  凶猛的； 强烈的
definition_en: done with a lot of energy and strong feelings, and sometimes violence
example: fierce fighting in the city

definition_zh: n  保留意见， 存疑； 预定， 预订
definition_en: an arrangement which you make so that a place in a hotel, restaurant, plane etc is kept for you at a particular time in the future
example: a dinner reservation

definition_zh: n 一排房屋，街区；阻塞；大块木料; vt  阻塞， 拦阻； 封锁
definition_en: a piece of hard material such as wood or stone with straight sides; to prevent anything moving through a space by being or placing something across it or in it
example: a wall made of concrete blocks

definition_zh: n  灭火器； 灭火者
definition_en: a fire extinguisher
example: Check the expiry date for all foam extinguisher.

definition_zh: n  合作， 协作； 配合
definition_en: when you work with someone to achieve something that you both want
example: the need to strengthen international co-operation

definition_zh: adj  胆小的； 羞怯的
definition_en: not having courage or confidence
example: I was a timid child.

definition_zh: v  提议， 建议； 提名， 推荐； 打算； 求婚
definition_en: to suggest something as a plan or course of action
example: the changes currently proposed by the local planning authorities

definition_zh: adj  休耕的； 休闲的
definition_en: fallow land is dug or  ploughed  but is not used for growing crops
example: They let the land lie fallow for a year.

definition_zh: adj  可更新的， 可再生的， 可恢复的； 可延长有效期的， 可续期的
definition_en: if an agreement or official document is renewable, you can make it continue for a further period of time after it ends
example: It’s a six-month lease but it’s renewable.

definition_zh: adj  有恶意的； 恶毒的
definition_en: a malevolent person wants to harm other people
example: Her stare was malevolent, her mouth a thin line.

definition_zh: vi  继而发生； 接着发生
definition_en: to happen after or as a result of something
example: If the Europeans did not reduce subsidies, a trade war would ensue.

definition_zh: adj  可预知的， 能预测的
definition_en: foreseeable difficulties, events etc should be planned for because they are very likely to happen in the future
example: The judge found that it was not foreseeable that the fuel would catch fire.

definition_zh: n 教练；指导；长途汽车; vt  训练； 辅导
definition_en: someone who trains a person or team in a sport; to teach a person or team the skills they need for a sport
example: a tennis coach

definition_zh: v 难过，
definition_en: to become sad and not continue your life as normal because someone has died or gone away
example: Six months after he left, she was still pining.

definition_zh: n  行李式传送带
definition_en: the moving belt that you collect your bags from at an airport

definition_zh: n  反话， 讽刺； 出人意料的事情
definition_en: a situation that is unusual or amusing because something strange happens, or the opposite of what is expected happens or is true
example: Life is full of little ironies.

definition_zh: n 碎屑; vt  废弃； 抛弃
definition_en: a small piece of paper, cloth etc; to decide not to use a plan or system because it is not practical
example: a rug made out of old scraps of material

definition_zh: n  连接； 连词
definition_en: a word such as ‘and’, ‘but’, or ‘because’ which joins parts of a sentence

definition_zh: adj  院的
definition_en: of or relating to a judicial or legislative chamber

definition_zh: n  草地
definition_en: a field with wild grass and flowers
example: Lambs gamboled in the meadow.

definition_zh: adj 烤过的; v  烤， 炙; n 烤肉
definition_en: roasted; to cook something, such as meat, in an  oven  or over a fire, or to cook in this way; a large piece of roasted meat
example: roast chicken

definition_zh: n  连续； 接替， 继承
definition_en: happening one after the other without anything diffe-rent happening in between
example: She won the championship four times in succession.

definition_zh: vt  用力拖； 搬运
definition_en: to pull something heavy with a continuous steady movement
example: the steam locomotive which hauled the train

definition_zh: adj  有力的， 精力旺盛的； 积极的
definition_en: having or needing a lot of energy or determination
example: an energetic man

definition_zh: vt 有帮助，有益，
n  消耗量； 损耗； 减员
definition_en: when something is lost or destroyed, especially in a way that is not useful or reasonable, or the amount that is lost or destroyed
example: The system used to result in a great deal of food wastage.

definition_zh: vt  使贫困； 使枯竭， 使贫瘠
definition_en: to make someone very poor
example: Falling coffee prices have impoverished many developing economies.

definition_zh: n  质量， 才干， 能力； 口径
definition_en: the level of quality or ability that someone or something has achieved
example: The school attracts a high calibre of student.

definition_zh: adj  首要的， 第一位的； 最著名的; n 总理；首相
definition_en: best or most important; a  prime minister  – used in news reports
example: one of Dublin’s premier hotels

definition_zh: adj  受崇敬的； 神圣的
definition_en: holy or made holy by religious practices
example: The bones will be buried in hallowed ground.

definition_zh: adj  智力的； 理智的; n 知识分子
definition_en: relating to the ability to understand things and think intelligently; an intelligent, well-educated person who spends time thinking about complicated ideas and discussing them
example: Mark’s very intellectual.

definition_zh: vt  纠正； 要求归还， 收回； 开垦
definition_en: to get back an amount of money that you have paid
example: You may be entitled to reclaim some tax.

definition_zh: n  过敏的， 变态反应的， 变应性的
v 欺骗；作弊; n  欺骗； 骗子
definition_en: to behave in a dishonest way in order to win or to get an advantage, especially in a competition, game, or examination; someone who is dishonest and cheats
example: He had cheated in the test by using a calculator.

definition_zh: vt  使丧失能力， 使伤残； 使不能正常运转， 使无效
definition_en: to make someone unable to use a part of their body properly
example: Carter was permanently disabled in the war.

definition_zh: adv  毋庸置疑地， 确凿地
vt  使恐惧， 使惊骇； 使震惊； 使感到厌恶
definition_en: to make someone feel very shocked and upset or afraid
example: Henry was horrified by what had happened.

definition_zh: n  短故事； 趣闻， 轶事
definition_en: a short story based on your personal experience
example: The book is full of amusing anecdotes about his life in Japan.

definition_zh: n  奢侈， 挥霍； 放肆的言行
definition_en: Extravagance is the spending of more money than is reasonable or than you can afford
example: When the company went under, tales of his extravagance surged through the industry.

definition_zh: v  悄悄进入， 潜入； 渗入， 渗透
definition_en: to secretly join an organization or enter a place in order to find out information about it or harm it
example: Police attempts to infiltrate neo-Nazi groups were largely unsuccessful.

definition_zh: adj  敌方的； 不友善的； 不利的
definition_en: angry and deliberately unfriendly towards someone, and ready to argue with them
example: Southampton fans gave their former coach a hostile reception .

definition_zh: v  给…加燃料; n 燃料
definition_en: if you fuel a vehicle, or if it fuels up, fuel is put into it; a substance such as coal, gas, or oil that can be burned to produce heat or energy
example: We’d better fuel up at the next town.

definition_zh: n  一切植物， 植物群
definition_en: all the plants that grow in a particular place or country
example: Tourism is damaging the flora and fauna (= plants and animals ) of the island.

definition_zh: adj  天体的， 天上的
definition_en: relating to the sky or heaven
example: ...the clusters of celestial bodies in the ever-expanding universe.

definition_zh: vi 爬，爬行；缓慢行进; n  缓慢的行进； 自由泳
definition_en: to move along on your hands and knees with your body close to the ground; a very slow speed
example: We crawled into bed at 2 am.

definition_zh: adj  完全相同的， 同一的
definition_en: exactly the same, or very similar
example: four identical houses

definition_zh: v 走得慢；落后; n  间隔； 滞后
definition_en: to move or develop more slowly than others; a delay or period of waiting between one event and a second event
example: Western banks still lag behind financial institutions in most other regions of the country.

definition_zh: adj  有抱负的， 有雄心的， 有野心的
definition_en: determined to be successful, rich, powerful etc
example: Alfred was intensely ambitious, obsessed with the idea of becoming rich.

definition_zh: adj 学院的；学术的；不切实际的; n  学者； 大学教师
definition_en: relating to education, especially at college or university level; a teacher in a college or university
example: He possessed no academic qualifications.

definition_zh: adj  珊瑚色的， 红色的; n 珊瑚；珊瑚虫
definition_en: pink or reddish-orange in colour; a hard red, white, or pink substance formed from the bones of very small sea creatures, which is often used to make jewellery
example: It is like the bow of the King of the Persians that is painted with vermilion and is tipped with coral.

definition_zh: vi 运行，起作用; n  功能； 职责， 作用； 函数
definition_en: to work in the correct or intended way; the purpose that something has, or the job that someone or something does
example: Her legs have now ceased to function .

definition_zh: n  失职； 行为不良
definition_en: illegal or immoral behaviour or actions, especially by young people
example: the ever-rising statistics of delinquency and crime

definition_zh: adj  典型的， 有代表性的; n 代表，代理人
definition_en: typical of a particular group or thing; someone who has been chosen to speak, vote, or make decisions for someone else
example: The pollsters asked a representative sample of New York residents for their opinions.

definition_zh: n  经度， 经线
definition_en: the distance east or west of a particular  meridian (= imaginary line along the Earth’s surface from the North Pole to the South Pole )  , measured in degrees
example: The town lies at longitude 12˚ east.

definition_zh: adj  懒散的； 松弛的， 不严格的， 马虎的
definition_en: not strict or careful enough about standards of behaviour, work, safety etc
example: One of the problem areas is lax security for airport personnel.

definition_zh: n  比较， 对比， 对照； 比喻， 比拟
definition_en: the process of comparing two or more people or things
example: Her paintings invite comparison with those of the early Impressionists (= they remind you of them ) .

definition_zh: v 孵出，孵化；策划; n  开口； 舱门
definition_en: if an egg hatches, or if it is hatched, it breaks, letting the young bird, insect etc come out; a hole in a ship or aircraft, usually used for loading goods, or the door that covers it
example: The eggs take three days to hatch.

definition_zh: interjection 请重复一遍，请再说一遍〔没听清对方的话希望重复一遍时的用语〕; v 赦免; n 赦免令，赦免状
definition_en: used when you want someone to repeat something because you did not hear it; to officially allow someone who has been found guilty of a crime to go free without being punished; Pardon is also a noun
example: ‘Hurry up Jonathan!’ ‘Pardon?’ ‘I said hurry up!’

definition_zh: n  食草动物
definition_en: an animal that only eats plants
example: Most herbivore teeth are grinders, canines are usually absent.

definition_zh: n  冒险， 冒险活动； 异乎寻常的经历， 奇遇
definition_en: an exciting experience in which dangerous or unusual things happen
example: a great adventure

definition_zh: adj  连续的
definition_en: coming or following one after the other
example: The team has had five successive victories.

definition_zh: n 状况，状态；; vt  训练； 使适应， 使习惯于； 对…有重要影响
definition_en: the state that something is in, especially how good or bad its physical state is; to make a person or an animal think or behave in a certain way by influencing or training them over a period of time
example: Profits increased by £1.5m, despite the difficult economic conditions.

definition_zh: adj  口渴的； 饥渴的
definition_en: feeling that you want or need a drink
example: Can I have a glass of water? I’m really thirsty.

definition_zh: n  典礼； 仪式
definition_en: an important social or religious event, when a traditional set of actions is performed in a formal way
example: a wedding ceremony

definition_zh: v  改善， 改进
definition_en: to make something better, or to become better
example: a course for students wishing to improve their English

definition_zh: n  义务， 职责， 责任
definition_en: a moral or legal duty to do something
example: Employers have an obligation to treat all employees equally.

definition_zh: n  鹰， 隼
definition_en: a large bird that hunts and eats small birds and animals
example: She watched his every move like a hawk.

definition_zh: n  稀有； 稀有的事物
definition_en: something that is valuable or interesting because it is rare
example: Some of these plants are national rarities.

definition_zh: adj  爱国的， 有爱国心的； 显示出爱国精神的
definition_en: having or expressing a great love of your country
example: patriotic songs

definition_zh: n  盥洗室， 厕所
definition_en: a toilet or the room a toilet is in
example: ...the ladies' lavatory.

definition_zh: adj 外部的，外表的; n  外部， 外表
definition_en: on the outside of something; the outside of something, especially a building
example: The exterior walls need a new coat of paint.

definition_zh: vt&n  指责， 谴责， 责难
definition_en: If you censure someone for something that they have done, you tell them that you strongly disapprove of it
example: The ethics committee may take a decision to admonish him or to censure him.

definition_zh: adj  密闭的； 无懈可击的
definition_en: not allowing air to get in or out
example: airtight containers

definition_zh: n  技巧， 技艺； 技术， 技能
definition_en: a special way of doing something
example: He’s a great player, with brilliant technique.

definition_zh: n  速记法； 速记
definition_en: a fast method of writing using special signs or shorter forms to represent letters, words, and phrases
example: a secretary who takes shorthand (= writes in shorthand )

definition_zh: n  囚禁， 拘留
definition_en: when a person or animal is kept in a prison,  cage  etc and not allowed to go where they want
example: The hostages were released from captivity.

definition_zh: n  舞台布景； 风景， 景色
definition_en: the natural features of a particular part of a country that you can see, such as mountains, forests, deserts etc
example: The best part of the trip was the fantastic scenery.

definition_zh: adj  有效的， 生效的； 给人深刻印象的， 显著的
definition_en: successful, and working in the way that was intended
example: The cheaper drugs are just as effective in treating arthritis.

definition_zh: vt  粗暴地对待； 错误地处理， 胡乱操作
definition_en: to deal with a situation badly, because of a lack of skill or care
example: The prime minister admitted that the crisis had been mishandled.

definition_zh: adv  同样地， 类似地
definition_en: in a similar way
example: The first letter she wrote me was less than a page long, and her second letter was similarly brief.

definition_zh: n  肌肉； 体力； 力量， 实力
definition_en: one of the pieces of flesh inside your body that you use in order to move, and that connect your bones together
example: Relax your stomach muscles , then stretch again.

definition_zh: adj  慷慨的； 大量的
definition_en: someone who is generous is willing to give money, spend time etc, in order to help people or give them pleasure
example: a generous glass of wine

definition_zh: adj  不利的， 有害的
definition_en: causing harm or damage
example: the detrimental effect of pollution on the environment

definition_zh: n 方向；趋势，动向；
definition_en: the way something or someone moves, faces, or is aimed
example: Which direction did they go in ?

definition_zh: n  展览； 陈列
definition_en: a show of paintings, photographs, or other objects that people can go to see
example: ...an exhibition of expressionist art.

definition_zh: adv  两倍地， 双重地; adj 两倍的，双重的；两部分的
definition_en: two times as much or as many of something
example: a twofold increase in cases of TB

definition_zh: n  学士； 单身汉
definition_en: a man who has never been married
example: Gerald was 38, and a confirmed bachelor (=  a man who has decided that he will never marry  ) .

definition_zh: v 探索，查究；探查，探测; n  探针； 探测器， 探测飞船； 探索， 调查
definition_en: to ask questions in order to find things out, especially things that other people do not want you to know; a long thin metal instrument that doctors and scientists use to examine parts of the body
example: Police probed claims that he had sold drugs.

definition_zh: v  使充气， 使膨胀； 使得意， 使骄傲； 抬高， 使膨胀
definition_en: to fill something with air or gas so it becomes larger, or to become filled with air or gas
example: It took us half an hour to inflate the dinghy.

definition_zh: adj 稳定的，稳固的，安定的；沉稳的，持重的; n  马厩
definition_en: steady and not likely to move or change; a building where horses are kept
example: A wide base will make the structure much more stable.

definition_zh: vt 控制，利用；上马具; n  马具
definition_en: to control and use the natural force or power of something; a set of leather bands used to control a horse or to attach it to a vehicle it is pulling
example: We can harness the power of the wind to generate electricity.

definition_zh: vi  回答， 答复； 响应； 作出反应
definition_en: to do something as a reaction to something that has been said or done
example: They are likely to respond positively to the president's request for aid.

definition_zh: v 画素描；概述; n  素描； 速写
definition_en: to draw a sketch of something; a simple, quickly made drawing that does not show much detail
example: Holford sketched a 10-year programme for rebuilding the city.

definition_zh: n  发生的事； 事件， 事变
definition_en: an event, especially one that is unusual, important, or violent
example: Am I at risk because of some incident in my sexual past?

definition_zh: n  维持； 保养； 抚养费
definition_en: the repairs, painting etc that are necessary to keep something in good condition
example: the cost of repairs and maintenance

definition_zh: v  成群； 群集; n 串，束，群
definition_en: If people cluster together, they gather together in a small group; a group of things of the same kind that are very close together
example: The passengers clustered together in small groups.

definition_zh: vi  捕食； 折磨， 使烦恼； 掠夺; n 猎物，捕获物；牺牲品，战利品
definition_en: A creature that preys on other creatures lives by catching and eating them; an animal, bird etc that is hunted and eaten by another animal
example: The effect was to disrupt the food chain, starving many animals and those that preyed on them.

definition_zh: v  用力拖
n  远征， 探险， 考察； 旅行， 出行； 远征队， 探险队， 考察队
definition_en: a long and carefully organized journey, especially to a dangerous or unfamiliar place, or the people that make this journey
example: an expedition to the North Pole

definition_zh: adj  有脊椎的; n 脊椎动物
definition_en: a living creature that has a backbone
example: Dead bodies of vertebrate in the field should not be collected or closely examined.

definition_zh: n  音乐会， 演奏会
definition_en: a performance given by musicians or singers
example: a rock concert

definition_zh: adj  漠不关心的； 不烦恼的
definition_en: not worried about something because you think it does not affect you
example: Paul was unconcerned about what he had done.

definition_zh: v  吸收； 吸引…的注意， 使全神贯注； 把…并入， 同化
definition_en: to take in liquid, gas, or another substance from the surface or space around something
example: Plants absorb nutrients from the soil.

definition_zh: n 供给，供应；; vt  供给， 供应； 满足， 弥补
definition_en: an amount of something that is available to be used; to provide people with something that they need or want, especially regularly over a long period of time
example: The nation’s fuel supplies will not last forever.

definition_zh: vt 涉及；使关心；
definition_en: If you concern yourself with something, you give it attention because you think that it is important
example: I didn't concern myself with politics.

definition_zh: n  底土， 下层土
definition_en: the layer of soil between the ground’s surface and the lower layer of hard rock
example: ...the chalk subsoil on the site.

definition_zh: v 抑制，克制; n  反复句， 叠句； 副歌； 经常重复的评价
definition_en: to not do something that you want to do; part of a song or poem that is repeated, especially at the end of each  verse
example: Mrs. Hardie refrained from making any comment.

definition_zh: v  结合
definition_en: to combine, or to join things together to form one thing
example: The company plans to merge its subsidiaries in the US.

definition_zh: n 码头；船坞; v （使）〔船〕进港，（使）停靠码头
definition_en: a place in a port where ships are loaded, unloaded, or repaired; if a ship docks, or if the captain docks it, it sails into a dock so that it can unload
example: A crowd was waiting at the dock to greet them.

definition_zh: n 混杂，掺杂；
definition_en: a lot of different things mixed together in an untidy way, without any order
example: This might be compared with the jumble of wavelengths of sound that go to make up ordinary noise.

definition_zh: n  要素； 元素； [the -s] 基本原理
definition_en: one part or feature of a whole system, plan, piece of work etc, especially one that is basic or important
example: Business and management elements are built into the course.

definition_zh: n  财富， 富裕； 丰富， 富饶
adj  很可能的， 大概的
definition_en: likely to exist, happen, or be true
example: The probable cause of the fire was faulty wiring.

definition_zh: n  对立面， 对立物
definition_en: a person or thing that is as different as possible from someone or something else
example: She’s quite shy, the exact opposite of Becky.

definition_zh: n  营养障碍； 营养不良
example: In fleck corneal dystrophy, the lesion only involved stroma, epithelium and endothelium levels were normal.

definition_zh: v 打包，收拾；包装，包裹；塞满; n  包
definition_en: to put things into cases, bags etc ready for a trip somewhere; something wrapped in paper or packed in a box and then sent by post or taken somewhere
example: I forgot to pack my razor.

definition_zh: n  污染物质， 有害物质
definition_en: a substance that makes air, water, soil etc dangerously dirty, and is caused by cars, factories etc
example: a dumping ground for toxic pollutants

definition_zh: n  暗示， 提示
definition_en: an action or event that is a signal for something else to happen
example: She stood nervously in the wings waiting for her cue.

definition_zh: v  入学； 登记； 加入； 招收
v  侵犯， 侵害； 违背， 触犯； 干涉， 干扰
definition_en: If someone infringes a law or a rule, they break it or do something that disobeys it
example: The film exploited his image and infringed his copyright.

definition_zh: v  稳定， 稳固
example: However, after three weeks of cold exposure, body weight began to stabilise.

definition_zh: n  招待， 款待； 娱乐； 供消遣的事物
definition_en: when you entertain someone at home, or for business
example: the entertainment of friends

definition_zh: n 公文，文件，证件; vt  用文件证明； 记载
definition_en: a piece of paper that has official information on it; to write about something, film it, or take photographs of it, in order to record information about it
example: She produces legal documents for a downtown Seattle law firm.

definition_zh: n  可利用性， 可得性； 利用的可能性； 可利用的人或物
n  电池， 蓄电池
definition_en: an object that provides a supply of electricity for something such as a radio, car, or toy
example: You have to take the top off to change the batteries .

definition_zh: v  挨饿； 饿死
definition_en: to suffer or die because you do not have enough to eat
example: Thousands of people will starve if food doesn’t reach the city.

definition_zh: n  泥土， 黏土
definition_en: a type of heavy sticky earth that can be used for making pots, bricks etc
example: ...the heavy clay soils of Georgia.

definition_zh: vt  促进； 提升； 促销
definition_en: If people promote something, they help or encourage it to happen, increase, or spread
example: You don't have to sacrifice environmental protection to promote economic growth.

definition_zh: n&v  进攻； 抨击； 突然发作
definition_en: an act of violence that is intended to hurt a person or damage a place
example: a bomb attack

definition_zh: n  幽默， 幽默感
definition_en: the ability or tendency to think that things are funny, or funny things you say that show you have this ability
example: his humour and charm

definition_zh: vt  分发， 分配， 分送； 散布， 分布； 分类， 分区
definition_en: to share things among a group of people, especially in a planned way
example: a man distributing leaflets to passers-by

definition_zh: v  系， 贴； 认为有重要性； 使附属， 附加
definition_en: to allow something to happen, but only if someone agrees to do a particular thing or accept a particular idea
example: When approving a merger, the commission can attach conditions.

definition_zh: adj  文字的； 生动的， 形象的； 绘画的， 图表的
definition_en: Graphic means concerned with drawing or pictures, especially in publishing, industry, or computing
example: ...fine and graphic arts.

definition_zh: n  创造； 作品
definition_en: the act of creating something
example: a job creation scheme

definition_zh: n  法律， 法规； 立法
definition_en: a law or set of laws
example: This is a very important piece of legislation (= law ).

definition_zh: n  爱好； 趋势； 倾斜
definition_en: a tendency to think or behave in a particular way
example: My natural inclination was to say no.

definition_zh: adj  充满…的； 担心的， 烦恼的
definition_en: If a situation or action is fraught with problems or risks, it is filled with them
example: The earliest operations employing this technique were fraught with dangers.

definition_zh: n  组成部分， 成分； 原料； 要素， 因素
definition_en: one of the foods that you use to make a particular food or dish
example: Combine all the ingredients in a large bowl.

definition_zh: vi  参加， 参与
definition_en: to take part in an activity or event
example: Some members refused to participate.

definition_zh: adj  有立体视觉的； 有立体效果的; v  刺， 戳； 黏贴， 黏住； 卡住， 陷住
definition_en: a stereoscopic picture, photograph etc appears solid when you look at it through a special machine
example: {"COLLOINEXA":["stereoscopic vision"]}

definition_zh: adj  节约的； 经济的
definition_en: using money, time, goods etc carefully and without wasting any
example: A small car is more economical to run.

definition_zh: v 磨光，擦亮；使优美，
definition_en: to make something smooth, bright, and shiny by rubbing it
example: I spent all afternoon polishing the silver.

definition_zh: adj  视力的； 光学的
definition_en: relating to machines or processes which are concerned with light, images, or the way we see things
example: microscopes and other optical instruments

definition_zh: n  方式； 流行款式； 时装
definition_en: a style of clothes, hair etc that is popular at a particular time
example: Young people are very concerned with fashion

definition_zh: n  保险； 保险费； 保险业
definition_en: an arrangement with a company in which you pay them money, especially regularly, and they pay the costs if something bad happens, for example if you become ill or your car is damaged
example: Your father took out insurance to cover the mortgage.

definition_zh: v 使目眩；
definition_en: if a very bright light dazzles you, it stops you from seeing properly for a short time
example: a deer dazzled by the headlights

definition_zh: n  原则， 原理； 规范， 准则； 基本信念， 信条
definition_en: a moral rule or belief about what is right and wrong, that influences how you behave
example: Schools try to teach children a set of principles.

definition_zh: adj  医学的， 医疗的； 内科的
definition_en: relating to medicine and the treatment of disease or injury
example: medical research

definition_zh: n  暗室
definition_en: a special room with only red light or no light, where film from a camera is made into photographs
example: Through controlling the each step in process of darkroom disposal, improve the quality of the sciagram efficently.

definition_zh: n  大腿
definition_en: the top part of your leg, between your knee and your  hip
example: chicken thighs

definition_zh: adj  冗长乏味的， 单调的
definition_en: something that is tedious continues for a long time and is not interesting
example: The work was tiring and tedious.

definition_zh: n  来源， 出处； 源头， 发源地； 根源， 起源
definition_en: a thing, place, activity etc that you get something from
example: They get their money from various sources.

definition_zh: v  散发…的气味； 闻到， 嗅到
definition_en: to notice or recognize a particular smell
example: I can smell burning.

definition_zh: n  参加者， 参与者
definition_en: someone who is taking part in an activity or event
example: 40 of the course participants are offered employment with the company.

definition_zh: adj  本国的； 家庭的； 驯养的
definition_en: relating to or happening in one particular country and not involving any other countries
example: Unfortunately his domestic life wasn’t very happy.

definition_zh: vt  开始， 创造， 发起； 接纳， 让…加入; n  加入组织者
definition_en: to arrange for something important to start, such as an official process or a new plan
example: They have decided to initiate legal proceedings against the newspaper.

definition_zh: n  领地， 庄园
definition_en: a big old house with a large area of land around it
example: The castle dates from the 1500s and has a rustic charm, reminiscent of a grand West Country manor.

definition_zh: adj 净化的，清洁的; n  清洁剂
definition_en: a liquid or powder used for washing clothes, dishes etc
example: During dry place, keep within the new air circulation, avoid rain and insolate, also can use some detergent to remove odor.

definition_zh: adj  陆地的， 陆生的， 陆栖的； 地球的
definition_en: relating to the Earth rather than to the moon or other  planets
example: ...terrestrial life forms.

definition_zh: n  灌溉； 冲洗
example: Small blockages or collapses of the canal would choke the intricate irrigation networks.

definition_zh: vt  汇编； 编纂
definition_en: to make a book, list, record etc, using different pieces of information, music etc
example: The document was compiled by the Department of Health.

definition_zh: n  欺骗； 诡计
definition_en: the act of deliberately making someone believe something that is not true
example: She didn’t have the courage to admit to her deception.

definition_zh: n  生命力， 活力
definition_en: great energy and eagerness to do things
example: Despite her eighty years, Elsie was full of vitality.

definition_zh: n  捐款； 捐赠
definition_en: something, especially money, that you give to a person or an organ-ization in order to help them
example: There have been generous donations from EU funds.

definition_zh: vt  使熟悉， 使通晓； 使家喻户晓
v  做预算; n 预算，预算经费
definition_en: to carefully plan and control how much money you spend and what you will buy with it; the money that is available to an organization or person, or a plan of how it will be spent
example: We’ll have to budget more carefully.

definition_zh: v  发光； 发热； 辐射
definition_en: if something radiates light or heat, or if light or heat radiates from something, the light or heat is sent out in all directions
example: The log fire radiated a warm cosy glow.

definition_zh: adj  实际的； 真实的
definition_en: used to emphasize that something is real or exact
example: I’m not joking. Those were his actual words.

definition_zh: n  胸； 金库， 资金
definition_en: Your chest is the top part of the front of your body where your ribs, lungs, and heart are
example: He crossed his arms over his chest.

definition_zh: n  校园
definition_en: the land and buildings of a university or college, including the buildings where students live
example: a beautiful campus in New England

definition_zh: adj 最后的，最终的；根本的; n  最好的事物， 极品， 精华
definition_en: someone’s ultimate aim is their main and most important aim, that they hope to achieve in the future; the best or most modern example of something
example: The ultimate outcome of the experiment cannot be predicted.

definition_zh: adj  腐蚀性的； 讽刺的， 挖苦的; n 腐蚀剂
definition_en: a caustic substance can burn through things by chemical action
example: caustic soda (= a chemical used for cleaning things )

definition_zh: adj  口头的； 言辞的， 文字的， 词语的； 动词的
definition_en: spoken rather than written
example: verbal skills

definition_zh: v  发展； 生长， 形成； 开发
definition_en: to grow or change into something bigger, stronger, or more advanced, or to make someone or something do this
example: Knowledge in the field of genetics has been developing very rapidly.

definition_zh: adj  祖先的； 祖传的
definition_en: You use ancestral to refer to a person's family in former times, especially when the family is important and has property or land that they have had for a long time
example: ...the family's ancestral home in southern Germany.

definition_zh: n  区， 地区
definition_en: an area of a town or the countryside, especially one with particular features
example: a postal district

definition_zh: adj  树木的； 栖于树木的
definition_en: relating to trees, or living in trees
example: ...arboreal marsupials which resemble monkeys.

definition_zh: adj  全面的； 全部的
definition_en: considering or including everything
example: The overall cost of the exhibition was £400,000.

definition_zh: vt  假定， 设想； 承担， 就
definition_en: to think that something is true, although you do not have definite proof
example: I think we can safely assume (=  it is almost certain  ) that interest rates will go up again soon.

definition_zh: n 胡椒; vt  在…上撒
definition_en: a powder that is used to add a hot taste to food; if something is peppered with things, it has a lot of those things in it or on it
example: salt and pepper

definition_zh: n  命运， 定数
definition_en: the things that will happen to someone in the future, especially those that cannot be changed or controlled
example: She always had a strong sense of destiny .

definition_zh: adj  博士的
definition_en: done as part of work for the university degree of doctor
example: a doctoral thesis

definition_zh: n  概念， 观念； 设想
definition_en: an idea of how something is, or how something should be done
example: a new concept in business travel

definition_zh: adj  抵抗的， 有抵抗力的
definition_en: not damaged or affected by something
example: Some people are very resistant to the idea of exercise.

definition_zh: n  一千年， 千禧年； 太平盛世
definition_en: a period of 1,000 years
example: people who have inhabited this land for millennia

definition_zh: adj  光秃秃的， 赤裸的； 荒凉的； 完全的
definition_en: very plain in appearance, with little or no colour or decoration
example: In the cold dawn light, the castle looked stark and forbidding.

definition_zh: n&vi  盯
definition_en: to look at something or someone for a long time without moving your eyes, for example because you are surprised, angry, or bored
example: She sat there staring into space (= looking for a long time at nothing ) .

definition_zh: adj  可退还的， 可归还的， 可偿还的
definition_en: A refundable payment will be paid back to you in certain circumstances
example: A refundable deposit is payable on arrival.

definition_zh: vt  提高， 增强， 增进
definition_en: to improve something
example: Good lighting will enhance any room.

definition_zh: n  大会， 会议； 惯例， 常规， 习俗； 公约， 协定
definition_en: a large formal meeting for people who belong to the same profession or organization or who have the same interests
example: a teachers’ convention

definition_zh: adj 防腐的，
example: The antiseptic properties of copper sulphate have never been questioned.

definition_zh: n  钙
definition_en: a silver-white metal that helps to form teeth, bones, and  chalk  . It is a chemical  element  : symbol Ca
example: Dairy source of protein and calcium.

definition_zh: n  拒绝
definition_en: when you say firmly that you will not do, give, or accept something
example: Her country suffered through her refusal to accept change.

definition_zh: vt 买，购买; n  购买的物品
definition_en: to buy something; something you buy, or the act of buying it
example: You can purchase insurance online.

definition_zh: n  滨， 岸， 海滩
definition_en: the land along the edge of a large area of water such as an ocean or lake
example: We could see a boat about a mile from shore.

definition_zh: vi  倾泻； 充满， 遍布
definition_en: If you say that a place is teeming with people or animals, you mean that it is crowded and the people and animals are moving around a lot
example: For most of the year, the area is teeming with tourists.

definition_zh: adj  时髦的； 漂亮的
definition_en: attractive in a fashionable way
example: a stylish woman in her forties

definition_zh: n 旁道; vt  绕过
definition_en: an operation to direct blood through new  vein s (=  blood tubes  )  outside the heart because the veins in the heart are blocked or diseased; to go around a town or other busy place rather than through it
example: a triple heart bypass operation

definition_zh: adj 相等的; vt  比得上
definition_en: the same in size, number, amount, value etc as something else; to be as good as something else, or get to the same standard as someone or something else
example: Our constitution states that all men are equal.

definition_zh: n  验光师， 视力测定者
definition_en: An optometrist is someone whose job is to test people's eyesight to see if they need glasses, and how strong their glasses should be
example: "I see what I would consider a normal patient population, representative of the average experience most people are having, " Brooklyn optometrist Justin Bazan, a paid consultant to the Vision Council.

definition_zh: n  硅
definition_en: a chemical substance that exists as a solid or as a powder and is used to make glass, bricks, and parts for computers. It is a chemical element: symbol Si
example: But the brittleness of silicon limits its uses.

definition_zh: n 基金，专款；储备，蕴藏；; vt  为…提供资金， 给…拨款
definition_en: an amount of money that is collected and kept for a particular purpose; to provide money for an activity, organization, event etc
example: The fund was set up to try to save the cathedral.

definition_zh: n  重力
definition_en: the force that causes something to fall to the ground or to be attracted to another  planet
example: the force of gravity

definition_zh: n  温和， 中庸； 适度， 合理
example: Some people think drinking in moderation can prevent heart disease.

definition_zh: n  接受者
definition_en: someone who receives something
example: ...the largest recipient of U.S. foreign aid.

definition_zh: n  [the C-] 英联邦； 联合体
definition_en: The commonwealth is an organization consisting of the United Kingdom and most of the countries that were previously under its rule
example: ...the Asian, Caribbean and African members of the commonwealth.

definition_zh: n&a  有袋动物
definition_en: an animal such as a  kangaroo  which carries its babies in a pocket of skin on its body
example: The smallest marsupial in all the world is but 45mm in length. So small that when fully grown it can stand comfortable in a dessert spoon.

definition_zh: adj 燃烧的; v 〔鸟或虫等〕飞落
definition_en: burning; if a bird or insect alights on something, it stops flying and stands on it
example: The car was set alight and pushed over a hill.

definition_zh: vt  给予…政治权利； 解放
definition_en: to give a group of people the right to vote
example: The company voted to enfranchise its 120 women members.

definition_zh: vt 为…提供资金; n 财政，金融；
definition_en: to provide money, especially a lot of money, to pay for something; the management of money by governments, large organizations etc
example: The concerts are financed by the Arts Council.

definition_zh: vt  藏匿； 贮藏
definition_en: to store something secretly or safely somewhere
example: We went for the bottle of whisky that we had stashed behind the bookcase.

definition_zh: vt  招致， 遭受
definition_en: if you incur something unpleasant, it happens to you because of something you have done
example: The government had also incurred huge debts.

definition_zh: vt  淹没， 泛滥； 使不胜负荷， 使应接不暇
definition_en: to receive so much of something that you cannot easily deal with it all
example: After the broadcast, we were inundated with requests for more information.

definition_zh: n  工作室； 摄影室； 练习室
definition_en: a room where a painter or photographer regularly works
example: a photographer’s studio

definition_zh: v  管制， 控制； 调节， 校准； 调整
definition_en: to control an activity or process, especially by rules
example: strict rules regulating the use of chemicals in food

definition_zh: adj  互补的； 补充的， 补足的
definition_en: complementary things go well together, although they are usually different
example: The computer and the human mind have different but complementary abilities.

definition_zh: n  腺
definition_en: an organ of the body which produces a substance that the body needs, such as hormones, sweat, or saliva
example: the pituitary gland

definition_zh: vt  使失去权力或影响
adj  可支付的； 应支付的
definition_en: a bill, debt etc that is payable must be paid
example: The rent is payable in advance .

definition_zh: n 前提，假设；
definition_en: a statement or idea that you accept as true and use as a base for developing other ideas
example: The idea that there is life on other planets is the central premise of the novel.

definition_zh: n  镇压， 压制， 抑制； 扑灭
adj  争论的
definition_en: causing a lot of disagreement, because many people have strong opinions about the subject being discussed
example: the controversial issue of welfare reform

definition_zh: n  支付； 支付的款项
definition_en: an amount of money that has been or must be paid
example: You can make a payment in any bank.

definition_zh: vt  达到； 获得； 完成
definition_en: to succeed in achieving something after trying for a long time
example: More women are attaining positions of power.

definition_zh: adj  无偏见的； 公正的
definition_en: not prejudiced or biased; impartial
example: Narrator: This is a tale about an unprejudiced heart, and how it changed our valley forever.

definition_zh: n  承租人； 房客； 佃户
definition_en: someone who lives in a house, room etc and pays rent to the person who owns it
example: The desk was left by the previous tenant.

definition_zh: n  美术馆
definition_en: a large building where people can see famous pieces of art
example: an exhibition of African art at the Hayward Gallery

definition_zh: n  代理人； 代表
definition_en: someone who is directly below another person in rank, and who is officially in charge when that person is not there
example: ...Jack Lang, France's minister for culture, and his deputy, Catherine Tasca.

definition_zh: n  不幸的人
definition_en: someone that you feel sorry for
example: He was a lonely, miserable wretch.

definition_zh: n  优先， 重点； 优先考虑的事
definition_en: the right to be given attention first and before other people or things
example: I want to start work on the garden but the house must take priority .

definition_zh: n  入口； 进口， 水湾
definition_en: a narrow area of water that reaches from the sea or a lake into the land
example: There are several sheltered inlets along the coast.

definition_zh: adj  创造性的； 创作的
definition_en: involving the use of imagination to produce new ideas or things
example: This job is so boring. I wish I could do something more creative.

definition_zh: n  航天飞机
definition_en: a  space shuttle
example: There is a free 24-hour shuttle between the airport terminals.

definition_zh: vt  分配； 分派
definition_en: to use something for a particular purpose, give something to a particular person etc, especially after an official decision has been made
example: Tickets are limited and will be allocated to those who apply first.

definition_zh: adj  最初的， 初级的； 首要的， 基本的
definition_en: most important
example: Our primary concern is to provide the refugees with food and health care.

definition_zh: n 浮石，浮岩; vt  用浮岩磨
definition_en: very light grey rock from a  volcano  that is crushed to a powder and used for cleaning
example: Some rocks, like gray volcanic pumice, are laced with air pockets created when gases expanded inside the rock while it was still molten.

definition_zh: n  内省， 反省
definition_en: the process of thinking deeply about your own thoughts, feelings, or behaviour
example: He had always had his moments of quiet introspection.

definition_zh: n 原木；航海或飞行日志; vt  记录
definition_en: a thick piece of wood from a tree; to make an official record of events, facts etc
example: a roaring log fire

definition_zh: adj  北欧人的； 北欧国家的
definition_en: relating to the northern European countries of Denmark, Norway, Sweden, Iceland, and Finland, or to their people
example: the Nordic countries

definition_zh: n  企业， 公司； 事业
definition_en: a company, organization, or business
example: commercial enterprises such as banks and food manufacturers

definition_zh: n  空气流通； 通风设备， 通风方法
example: I see no ventilation to speak of, none from a window or a ventilator at least.

definition_zh: adj  太阳的； 太阳能的
definition_en: relating to the sun
example: a solar eclipse

definition_zh: n  圆屋顶； 穹顶
definition_en: a round roof on a building
example: ...the dome of the Capitol.

definition_zh: n  电影摄影术
definition_en: the skill or study of making films
example: ...an admirer of Arthur Jafa's breathtaking cinematography.

definition_zh: v  因保险丝烧断而断电； 熔合； 熔化; n 保险丝
definition_en: if electrical equipment fuses, or if you fuse it, it stops working because a fuse has melted; a short thin piece of wire inside electrical equipment which prevents damage by melting and stopping the electricity when there is too much power
example: The lights have fused again.

definition_zh: adj 高级的，优质的；
definition_en: of very high quality
example: premium ice cream

definition_zh: vt  使确信， 使确保； 保证， 向…保证
definition_en: to tell someone that something will definitely happen or is definitely true so that they are less worried
example: The document is genuine, I can assure you .

definition_zh: v  请教； 查阅； 商议
definition_en: to ask for information or advice from someone because it is their job to know something
example: If symptoms persist, consult a doctor without delay.

definition_zh: n  团结， 一致
definition_en: loyalty and general agreement between all the people in a group, or between different groups, because they all have a shared aim
example: a gesture of solidarity

definition_zh: adv  仅仅， 只不过
definition_en: used to emphasize how small or unimportant something or someone is
example: He’s merely a boy – you can’t expect him to understand.

definition_zh: n  矛盾， 不一致； 否认， 反驳
definition_en: a difference between two statements, beliefs, or ideas about something that means they cannot both be true
example: apparent contradictions in the defendant’s testimony

definition_zh: v  活下来； 幸免于； 比…活得长， 比…长命
definition_en: to continue to live after an accident, war, or illness
example: Only 12 of the 140 passengers survived.

definition_zh: adj 最初的，开始的；词首的; n  词首大写字母
definition_en: happening at the beginning; the first letter of someone’s first name
example: an initial investment of £5,000

definition_zh: adj  基本的， 初步的； 未充分发展的， 发育不成熟的； 退化的
definition_en: a rudimentary knowledge or understanding of a subject is very simple and basic
example: Gradually, I acquired a rudimentary knowledge of music.

definition_zh: adj  不安全的， 充满危险的； 不牢靠的， 不稳固的
definition_en: a precarious situation or state is one which may very easily or quickly become worse
example: Her health remained precarious, despite the treatment.

definition_zh: adj  观念的， 概念的
definition_en: dealing with ideas, or based on them
example: the conceptual framework of the play

definition_zh: adj  永恒的
definition_en: continuing for ever and having no end
example: the Christian promise of eternal life

definition_zh: adv  仅仅， 几乎不； 赤裸裸地， 无遮蔽地
definition_en: almost not
example: She was barely aware of his presence.

definition_zh: adj  流行的， 普遍的
definition_en: common at a particular time, in a particular place, or among a particular group of people
example: the prevalent belief in astrology

definition_zh: v  夹住， 扣住； 剪， 修剪; n  夹子， 回形针， 别针； 弹夹； 修剪； 剪报， 电影片断
definition_en: to fasten something together or to be fastened together using a clip; a small metal or plastic object that holds or fastens things together
example: a stack of bills clipped together

definition_zh: adj  惨重的， 毁灭性的； 压倒的， 决定性的
definition_en: A crushing defeat, burden, or disappointment is a very great or severe one
example: ...since their crushing defeat in the local elections.

definition_zh: n  滋养物， 营养品
definition_en: a chemical or food that provides what is needed for plants or animals to live and grow
example: The plant absorbs nutrients from the soil.

definition_zh: adj  无价的
definition_en: extremely useful
example: The internet is an invaluable source of information.

definition_zh: adj 紧密的，结实的；简明的；紧凑的; vt  使紧凑， 压缩
definition_en: packed or put together firmly and closely; to press something together so that it becomes smaller or more solid
example: The bushes grew in a compact mass.

definition_zh: n 亡命之徒，逃犯，被剥夺法律权益者; vt  宣布…为非法， 使…成为非法
definition_en: someone who has done something illegal, and who is hiding in order to avoid punishment – used especially about criminals in the past; When something is outlawed, it is made illegal
example: Jesse was an outlaw, a bandit, a criminal.

definition_zh: vt 注定…失败，使…在劫难逃; n  毁灭； 厄运， 劫数
definition_en: to make someone or something certain to fail, die, be destroyed etc; something very bad that is going to happen, or the fact that it is going to happen
example: The plan was doomed from the start .

definition_zh: n  持有， 拥有； 所有权； 所有物， 财产， 财富； 领土
definition_en: if something is in your possession, you own it, or you have obtained it from somewhere
example: That information is not in our possession.

definition_zh: v  表示， 指示； 意味着
definition_en: to mean something
example: What does the word ‘curriculum’ denote that ‘course’ does not?

definition_zh: v  开花; n 花；开花；青春焕发
definition_en: if a plant or a flower blooms, its flowers appear or open; a flower or flowers
example: These plants bloom in spring.

definition_zh: n  地区， 地带； 区域
definition_en: a large area that is different from other areas around it in some way
example: San Francisco and Tokyo are both located in earthquake zones.

definition_zh: n  故事情节
definition_en: The storyline of a book, movie, or play is its story and the way in which it develops
example: The surprise twists in the storyline are the film's greatest strength.

definition_zh: v  繁殖， 增殖； 传播， 宣传， 使普及
definition_en: to spread an idea, belief etc to many people
example: The group launched a website to propagate its ideas .

definition_zh: adj  正统的， 传统的
definition_en: orthodox ideas, methods, or behaviour are accepted by most people to be correct and right
example: orthodox medical treatments

definition_zh: v  工业化
example: The way to bring cost down is to industrialise much of the process.

definition_zh: n  拱顶石； 基础， 主旨
definition_en: the large central stone in an arch that keeps the other stones in position
example: The government's determination to beat inflation has so far been the keystone of its economic policy.

definition_zh: n  结合， 联合； 化合
definition_en: two or more different things that exist together or are used or put together
example: a combination nightclub and café

definition_zh: v  获得， 得到； 通用， 流行
definition_en: to get something that you want, especially through your own effort, skill, or work
example: These conditions no longer obtain.

definition_zh: n  好奇， 好奇心； 稀奇或罕见的事物或人， 古玩
definition_en: the desire to know about something
example: I opened the packet just to satisfy my curiosity .

definition_zh: vt  碾磨成粉， 粉碎
example: Device used to crush or pulverise plastic or solid coating to powder form.

definition_zh: vt 使格式化; n  安排， 计划； 版式； 格式
definition_en: to organize the space on a computer  disk  so that information can be stored on it; the way in which something such as a television show or meeting is organized or arranged
example: better ways to format your spreadsheets

definition_zh: v  牺牲， 献祭; n 牺牲，牺牲品；献祭，供奉；祭品，供物
definition_en: to willingly stop having something you want or doing something you like in order to get something more important; when you decide not to have something valuable, in order to get something that is more important
example: The priest sacrificed a chicken.

definition_zh: adj 特殊的，特别的；特定的，个别的；详细的; n  详情， 细目， 细节
definition_en: a particular thing or person is the one that you are talking about, and not any other; the facts and details about a job, property, legal case etc
example: In this particular case, no one else was involved.

definition_zh: adj  不合法的， 违法的
definition_en: not allowed by the law
example: illegal drugs

definition_zh: n  部队； 军团
definition_en: a group in an army with special duties and responsibilities
example: the medical corps

definition_zh: vt 品尝，品味；欣赏；体会，体味; n  味道， 风味； 情趣， 吸引力
definition_en: to fully enjoy the taste or smell of something; a pleasant taste or smell
example: She sipped her wine, savouring every drop.

definition_zh: vt  祝贺
definition_en: to tell someone that you are happy because they have achieved something or because something nice has happened to them
example: He never even stopped to congratulate me.

definition_zh: adj  正式的； 正规的
definition_en: made or done officially or publicly
example: formal recognition of the reformed church

definition_zh: adj  草绿色的； 似草的
definition_en: covered with grass
example: a grassy bank

definition_zh: n  结合， 团结； 凝聚力
definition_en: if there is cohesion among a group of people, a set of ideas etc, all the parts or members of it are connected or related in a reasonable way to form a whole
example: a sense of community and social cohesion

definition_zh: v  欢乐， 娱乐； 招待
definition_en: If a performer, performance, or activity entertains you, it amuses you, interests you, or gives you pleasure
example: They were entertained by top singers, dancers and celebrities.

definition_zh: v  再生长， 再植
definition_en: (of plants) to grow again and produce new tissue, esp to produce new growth on bare ground

definition_zh: adj  批评的， 评论的； 危急的， 紧要的； 临界的； 挑剔的
definition_en: if you are critical, you criticize someone or something
example: He made some highly critical remarks.

definition_zh: adv  偶发地； 零星地
example: She gently dots it on to the face sporadically to mimic the pattern of real freckles.

definition_zh: adj 主要的; n  校长； 资本； 主角
definition_en: most important; someone who is in charge of a school
example: His principal reason for making the journey was to visit his family.

definition_zh: vt  做， 犯
definition_en: to do something that is morally wrong or illegal
example: Who could have perpetrated such a dreadful crime?

definition_zh: v  倾向于， 趋于； 照料； 招待
definition_en: to look after someone or something
example: Sofia was in the bedroom tending to her son.

definition_zh: n  范例， 样品； 样本， 标本
definition_en: a small amount or piece that is taken from something, so that it can be tested or examined
example: a blood specimen

definition_zh: adj  外来的； 奇异的； 醒目的， 吸引人的
definition_en: Something that is exotic is unusual and interesting, usually because it comes from or is related to a distant country
example: ...brilliantly coloured, exotic flowers.

definition_zh: n  减轻， 解除； 援救， 救济； 安慰； 缓合剂
definition_en: when something reduces someone’s pain or unhappy feelings
example: Marijuana can provide pain relief for some cancer patients.

definition_zh: n  介绍； 传入， 引进； 导言， 绪论
definition_en: the act of formally telling two people each other’s names when they first meet
example: Pete, are you going to make the introductions?

definition_zh: adj 草本植物的；药草的; n  草本植物志； 药草书
definition_en: Herbal means made from or using herbs
example: ...herbal remedies for colds.

definition_zh: vt  蒸馏， 提取
definition_en: to make a liquid such as water or alcohol more pure by heating it so that it becomes a gas and then letting it cool. Drinks such as  whisky  are made this way.
example: distilled water

definition_zh: v  订阅， 订购； 申请， 预订
definition_en: to pay money, usually once a year, to have copies of a newspaper or magazine sent to you, or to have some other service
example: My main reason for subscribing to New Scientist is to keep abreast of advances in science.

definition_zh: n  治疗， 疗法
definition_en: the treatment of an illness or injury over a fairly long period of time
example: new drug therapies

definition_zh: n  集中； 专心， 专注； 浓缩， 浓度
definition_en: the ability to think about something carefully or for a long time
example: She needed all her powers of concentration to stop herself from slipping on the icy road.

definition_zh: n  速度， 速率； 迅速， 快速
definition_en: the speed of something that is moving in a particular direction
example: the velocity of light

definition_zh: vi  同情， 怜悯
definition_en: to express your sympathy for someone who is unhappy about something
example: When I lost, he commiserated with me.

definition_zh: vi  干涉， 干扰， 妨碍
definition_en: to deliberately get involved in a situation where you are not wanted or needed
example: My daughter-in-law said that I was interfering, but I was only trying to help.

definition_zh: vt  装入胶囊； 压缩； 总结， 概述
definition_en: to express or show something in a short way
example: The words of the song neatly encapsulate the mood of the country at that time.

definition_zh: adj  适合的; n 试穿；装置
definition_en: right for a particular situation or occasion; an occasion when you put on a piece of clothing that is being made for you, to see if it fits properly
example: I thought the memorial was a fitting tribute to the President.

definition_zh: vt  容忍， 宽容； 容许， 承认
definition_en: to allow people to do, say, or believe something without criticizing or punishing them
example: We simply will not tolerate vigilante groups on our streets.

definition_zh: v  把…叠成堆， 堆放于； 堆积， 堆起; n 整齐的一叠
definition_en: to put neat piles of things on something
example: He went back to stacking the shelves.

definition_zh: vt  画线于…之下； 强调
definition_en: to draw a line under a word to show that it is important
example: This tragic incident underlines the need for immediate action.

definition_zh: v  愤恨； 对…表示愤恨， 感到气愤
definition_en: to feel angry or upset about a situation or about something that someone has done, especially because you think that it is not fair
example: Paul resented the fact that Carol didn’t trust him.

definition_zh: v 模拟，模仿; n  哑剧表演； 哑剧演员
definition_en: to pretend to play or sing a piece of music, without making any sound; the use of movements to express what you want to say without using words, or a play where the actors use only movements
example: The children learn through role-play, dance and mime.

definition_zh: n  职位低的人， 下属
example: ...underlings who do the dirty work.

definition_zh: n  议会， 国会
definition_en: the group of people who are elected to make a country’s laws and discuss important national affairs
example: They demanded a free parliament and press.

definition_zh: adj  含糊其辞的； 不明确的， 模棱两可的
definition_en: something that is ambiguous is unclear, confusing, or not certain, especially because it can be understood in more than one way
example: The language in the Minister’s statement is highly ambiguous.

definition_zh: v  破裂； 发出爆裂声； 重击； 崩溃， 瓦解; n 裂缝，缝隙；爆裂声
definition_en: to break or to make something break, either so that it gets lines on its surface, or so that it breaks into pieces; a very narrow space between two things or two parts of something
example: Don’t put boiling water in the glass or it will crack.

definition_zh: v  捆绑， 系； 约束； 凝结
