definition_en: if you are bound by an agreement, promise etc, you must do what you have agreed to do or promised to do
example: The monks are bound by vows of silence.

definition_zh: n  立方形； 立方
definition_en: a solid object with six equal square sides
example: a sugar cube

definition_zh: adj 脆的；利落的
definition_en: something that is crisp is hard, and makes a pleasant sound when you break it or crush it
example: She kicked at the crisp leaves at her feet.

definition_zh: v  凝结成块; n 凝块
definition_en: if a liquid such as blood or milk clots, or if something clots it, it becomes thicker and more solid; a thick almost solid mass formed when blood or milk dries
example: The patient's blood refused to clot.

definition_zh: adj  温柔的； 温和的； 轻微的
definition_en: fairly warm
example: We had an exceptionally mild winter last year.

definition_zh: n  影响， 作用； 冲击
definition_en: the effect or influence that an event, situation etc has on someone or something
example: The force of the impact knocked the breath out of her.

definition_zh: n 面部的侧影，侧面轮廓；传略，人物简介; vt  扼要介绍； 写传略
definition_en: a side view of someone’s head; to write or give a short description of someone or something
example: Dani has a lovely profile.

definition_zh: n  瘫痪， 麻痹， 中风
definition_en: the loss of the ability to move all or part of your body or feel things in it
example: paralysis of the lower body

definition_zh: n  宿舍
definition_en: a  dormitory
example: My dorm always has the best parties.

definition_zh: n  水桶
definition_en: an open container with a handle, used for carrying and holding things, especially liquids
example: She threw a bucket of water over them.

definition_zh: adj 资格较老的；年长的; n  较年长者； 毕业班学生
definition_en: having a higher position, level, or rank; a student in their last year of  high school  or university
example: the senior Democrat on the House committee

definition_zh: n  剂量， 一剂
definition_en: the amount of a medicine or a drug that you should take
example: I quite like Jamie in small doses (= in limited amounts but not a lot or often ) .

definition_zh: v 胡乱涂抹；弄脏，弄污；诽谤，诋毁；变得模糊不清，
definition_en: to tell an untrue story about someone important in order to make people lose respect for them – used especially in newspapers
example: an attempt to smear the party leadership

definition_zh: adj  可延伸的， 可展开的， 可扩张的
definition_en: Something that is extendable can be made longer
example: These were hung in place with extendable rods.

definition_zh: adj 相对的；比较的；有关的，相关的; n  亲属， 亲戚
definition_en: having a particular quality when compared with something else; a member of your family
example: The relative merits of both approaches have to be considered.

definition_zh: n  态度， 看法； 姿势
definition_en: the opinions and feelings that you usually have about something, especially when this is shown in your behaviour
example: As soon as they found out I was a doctor, their whole attitude changed.

definition_zh: adj 平均的；平常的，普通的; v  平均达到； 平均为; n 平均
definition_en: the average amount is the amount you get when you add together several quantities and divide this by the total number of quantities; to usually do something or usually happen a particular number of times, or to usually be a particular size or amount; the amount calculated by adding together several quantities, and then dividing this amount by the total number of quantities
example: The age of the candidates ranged from 29 to 49 with an average age of 37.

definition_zh: n  治疗； 对待
definition_en: something that is done to cure someone who is injured or ill
example: Just lately, Kyra has been giving me the silent treatment (= refusing to speak to me because she is angry with me ) .

definition_zh: adj  色盲的
definition_en: unable to see the difference between all or some colours
example: Sixteen times as many men are colour-blind as women.

definition_zh: v  比较； 对比
definition_en: to consider two or more things or people, in order to show how they are similar or different
example: The report compares the different types of home computer available.

definition_zh: adj  有影响力的； 有权势的
definition_en: having a lot of influence and therefore changing the way people think and behave
example: He had influential friends.

definition_zh: adj  透明的， 清澈的； 明显的； 易懂的
definition_en: if something is transparent, you can see through it
example: a transparent plastic container

definition_zh: vt 抓紧；掌握，全面理解; n  抓住； 支配； 理解
definition_en: to take and hold something firmly; the way you hold something or your ability to hold it
example: I grasped his arm firmly and led him away.

definition_zh: n  杂志； 日报； 日志
definition_en: a serious magazine produced for professional people or those with a particular interest
example: the British Medical Journal

definition_zh: adj  出众的； 有特色的
definition_en: having a special quality, character, or appearance that is different and easy to recognize
example: a rock band with a distinctive sound

definition_zh: n  作品； 写作， 作曲； 结构， 组成， 成分
definition_en: the way in which something is made up of different parts, things, or members
example: Some minerals have complex chemical compositions.

definition_zh: n  交付， 委托； 投递， 发送； 所托运的货物
definition_en: a quantity of goods that are sent somewhere, especially in order to be sold
example: The first consignment of food was flown in yesterday.

definition_zh: vt  使肥沃， 使多产； 施肥于； 使受精
n  火山
definition_en: a mountain with a large hole at the top, through which lava(= very hot liquid rock ) is sometimes forced out
example: Pompeii was destroyed when the volcano erupted in 79 AD.

definition_zh: n  取得； 获得物
definition_en: the process by which you gain knowledge or learn a skill
example: the acquisition of language

definition_zh: adj  严格的， 死板的； 刚硬的， 僵硬的
definition_en: rigid methods, systems etc are very strict and difficult to change
example: rigid and authoritarian methods of education

definition_zh: n  最后期限
definition_en: a date or time by which you have to do or complete something
example: I’m always working under pressure to meet deadlines (= finish something on time ) .

definition_zh: n  异常； 偏常行为
adj  感激的， 感谢的
definition_en: feeling that you want to thank someone because of something kind that they have done, or showing this feeling
example: Our grateful thanks go to all who participated.

definition_zh: n  零售商人； 复述者， 传播者
definition_en: A retailer is a person or business that sells goods to the public
example: Furniture and carpet retailers are among those reporting the sharpest annual decline in sales.

definition_zh: n  到来； 出现
definition_en: the time when something first begins to be widely used
example: the advent of the computer

definition_zh: adj  中世纪的； 中古的
definition_en: connected with the Middle Ages (= the period between about 1100 and 1500 AD )
example: These spices were first brought to Italy from the East in medieval times.

definition_zh: adj  运转的； 操作的； 运营的， 业务的
definition_en: relating to the operation of a business, government etc
example: Patco accepted full responsibility for operational management.

definition_zh: n 全体职工，工作人员；棒；全体参谋人员; vt  为…配备人员； 任职于
definition_en: the people who work for an organization; to be or provide the workers for an organization
example: The entire staff has done an outstanding job this year.

definition_zh: vt  使卷入， 使参与； 牵涉， 陷入， 连累； 包含， 含有
definition_en: if an activity or situation involves something, that thing is part of it or a result of it
example: What will the job involve?

definition_zh: n  油轮； 油罐车； 加油飞机
definition_en: a vehicle or ship specially built to carry large quantities of gas or liquid, especially oil
example: A Greek oil tanker has run aground.

definition_zh: adj 需要的；必要的; n  必需品； 要素
definition_en: needed for a particular purpose; A requisite is something that is necessary for a particular purpose
example: He lacks the requisite qualifications.

definition_zh: n  舞台； 戏剧； 阶段
definition_en: a particular time or state that something reaches as it grows or develops
example: We’re getting to the stage where we hardly ever go out together.

definition_zh: adj  复杂的； 难懂的
definition_en: difficult to understand or deal with, because many parts or details are involved
example: a complicated voting system

definition_zh: vt  代替， 取代
definition_en: if a new idea, product, or method supersedes another one, it becomes used instead because it is more modern or effective
example: Their map has since been superseded by photographic atlases.

definition_zh: adj  最大的， 最高的; n 最大量，极限
definition_en: the maximum amount, quantity, speed etc is the largest that is possible or allowed; the largest number or amount that is possible or is allowed
example: The car has a maximum speed of 120 mph.

definition_zh: adj 沙漠的；荒凉的[dI'zɜːt]; v  舍弃; n 沙漠；荒地
definition_en: to leave someone or something and no longer help or support them; a large area of land where it is always very dry, and there is a lot of sand
example: Helen was deserted by her husband.

definition_zh: n  方言， 土语
definition_en: a form of a language which is spoken only in one area, with words or grammar that are slightly different from other forms of the same language
example: the local dialect

definition_zh: n  紧急情况， 突发事件
definition_en: an unexpected and dangerous situation that must be dealt with immediately
example: Lifeguards are trained to deal with emergencies.

definition_zh: n  才能； 学院， 系； 全体教学人员
definition_en: a department or group of related departments within a university
example: the Engineering Faculty

definition_zh: n  护照； 途径， 路子， 手段
definition_en: a small official document that you get from your government, that proves who you are, and which you need in order to leave your country and enter other countries
example: I have an Irish passport.

definition_zh: n  石灰石， 石灰岩
definition_en: a type of rock that contains calcium
example: ...high limestone cliffs.

definition_zh: n  魅力； 诱惑力
example: This is the power of glamor of debate!

definition_zh: n  露营者， 宿营者
definition_en: someone who is staying in a tent or shelter
example: My fellow campers were already packing up their tents.

definition_zh: n  线索； 提示
definition_en: an object or piece of information that helps someone solve a crime or mystery
example: Police have found a vital clue .

definition_zh: n  〈总称〉 家畜， 牲畜
definition_en: animals such as cows and sheep that are kept on a farm
example: The heavy rains and flooding killed scores of livestock.

definition_zh: adj  法律的； 合法的
definition_en: if something is legal, you are allowed to do it or have to do it by law
example: What the company has done is perfectly legal.

definition_zh: n  中间
definition_en: in a particular group
example: I fear we have an enemy in our midst.

definition_zh: n  公路
definition_en: a wide main road that joins one town to another
example: Interstate Highway 75

definition_zh: n  半径； 周围， 范围
definition_en: the distance from the centre to the edge of a circle, or a line drawn from the centre to the edge
example: The shock of the explosion was felt over a radius of forty miles.

definition_zh: adj  熔化的
definition_en: molten metal or rock has been made into a liquid by being heated to a very high temperature
example: molten iron

definition_zh: v  发信号； 标志着; n 信号；暗号；标志
definition_en: to make a sound or action in order to give information or tell someone to do something; a sound or action that you make in order to give information to someone or tell them to do something
example: She signalled, and the waiter brought the bill.

definition_zh: vt  损害； 削弱
definition_en: to damage something or make it not as good as it should be
example: The illness had impaired his ability to think and concentrate.

definition_zh: n  包裹
definition_en: an object that has been wrapped in paper or put in a special envelope, especially so that it can be sent by post
example: The parcel was delivered last week.

definition_zh: n 静电；[-s]
definition_en: noise caused by electricity in the air that blocks or spoils the sound from radio or TV
example: When the weather turns cold and dry, my clothes develop a static problem.

definition_zh: adj  自身免疫的
definition_en: Autoimmune describes medical conditions in which normal cells are attacked by the body's immune system
example: ...autoimmune diseases such as rheumatoid arthritis.

definition_zh: v  使非常疲倦， 使疲惫不堪； 用尽， 耗尽; n  废气， 蒸汽
definition_en: to use all of something; the gas produced when an engine is working
example: We are in danger of exhausting the world’s oil supply.

definition_zh: adj  可溶的； 可解决的
definition_en: a soluble substance can be  dissolved  in a liquid
example: soluble aspirin

definition_zh: n  节省； 削减
definition_en: Retrenchment means spending less money
example: Defence planners predict an extended period of retrenchment.

definition_zh: n  定罪； 坚信， 确信
definition_en: a very strong belief or opinion
example: It was a reasonable explanation, but his voice lacked conviction .

definition_zh: n 带子; vt  捆扎； 包扎
definition_en: a narrow band of strong material that is used to fasten, hang, or hold onto something; to tie  bandage s  firmly round a part of your body that has been hurt
example: a bra strap

definition_zh: n  稻草； 吸管
definition_en: a thin tube of paper or plastic for sucking up liquid from a bottle or a cup
example: She sipped her lemonade through a straw.

definition_zh: v  多样化
definition_en: if a business, company, country etc diversifies, it increases the range of goods or services it produces
example: We need to diversify the economy.

definition_zh: adj  发明的， 创造的； 善于发明创造的
definition_en: able to think of new, different, or interesting ideas
example: one of the most talented and inventive drummers in modern music

definition_zh: n  图， 图表， 曲线图
definition_en: a drawing that uses a line or lines to show how two or more sets of measurements are related to each other
example: Martin showed me a graph of their recent sales.

definition_zh: prep  关于
definition_en: a word used especially in letters or speeches to introduce the subject you are writing or talking about
example: Regarding your recent inquiry, I have enclosed a copy of our new brochure.

definition_zh: n  汽油
definition_en: a liquid obtained from  petroleum  that is used to supply power to the engine of cars and other vehicles
example: unleaded petrol

definition_zh: v  把遣返回国； 归国
definition_en: to send someone back to their own country
example: After the war, prisoners were repatriated.

definition_zh: adj  自命不凡的， 自负的； 炫耀的； 做作的
definition_en: if someone or something is pretentious, they try to seem more important, intelligent, or high class than they really are in order to be impressive
example: a pretentious film

definition_zh: v 对付，处理；向某人提起；抢截，抢球; n  用具， 器具； 滑轮， 滑车； 抢截
definition_en: to try to deal with a difficult problem; ropes and  pulleys (= wheels )  used for lifting heavy things
example: There is more than one way to tackle the problem.

definition_zh: adj  大规模的， 大范围的
definition_en: using or involving a lot of effort, people, supplies etc
example: a large-scale rescue operation

definition_zh: vi 逃避责任；旷课; n  逃学者； 逃避者
definition_en: a student who stays away from school without permission
example: persistent truants

definition_zh: vt  转移； 使分心
definition_en: to take someone’s attention away from something by making them look at or listen to something else
example: Try not to distract the other students.

definition_zh: n  流行性感冒
definition_en: an infectious disease that is like a very bad cold
example: All the medical workers in the region helped to put down the influenza epidemic.

definition_zh: n 时刻，时候；场合；重大活动，盛会；时机，机会；起因，理由; vt  引起， 惹起
definition_en: an important social event or ceremony; to cause something
example: I’m saving this bottle of champagne for a special occasion.

definition_zh: v 压榨，榨取；捏，挤压；挤入，挤过；向…勒索; n  挤压， 捏； 紧缺， 拮据， 经济困难； 减少， 削减
definition_en: to press something firmly together with your fingers or hand; an act of pressing something firmly with your fingers or hand
example: She smiled as he squeezed her hand.

definition_zh: n&vt  贷款， 借
definition_en: an amount of money that you borrow from a bank etc
example: I had to take out a loan to buy my car.

definition_zh: adj  有价值的； 值得的
definition_en: deserving respect from people
example: Leeds United were worthy winners of the competition.

definition_zh: v  狂饮， 暴饮； 大量消耗
definition_en: to eat or drink a lot of something, eagerly and quickly – usually showing disapproval
example: They’ve been guzzling beer all evening.

definition_zh: n 期待，预期；
definition_en: what you think or hope will happen
example: Having raised expectations (= made people more hopeful ), he went on to help only a few people.

definition_zh: n  变质； 损坏
definition_en: waste resulting from something being spoiled
example: Monitors and investigates spoilage and recommends appropriate actions to reduce.

definition_zh: n  药剂师， 配药师； 分配者， 施予者； 自动售货机
definition_en: a machine which provides a particular amount of a product or substance when you press a button or put money into it
example: a paper towel dispenser

definition_zh: n  持续， 持续的时间， 期间
definition_en: the length of time that something continues
example: The course is of three years’ duration.

definition_zh: v 装载；装; n  负荷； 装载
definition_en: to put a large quantity of something into a vehicle or container; a large quantity of something that is carried by a vehicle, person etc
example: Have you finished loading up?

definition_zh: n  久经世故的人； 精通者
definition_en: someone who is sophisticated

definition_zh: v  渡运; n 渡船；摆渡
definition_en: to carry people or things a short distance from one place to another in a boat or other vehicle; a boat that carries people or goods across a river or a narrow area of water
example: Every day, a plane arrives to ferry guests to and from Bird Island Lodge.

definition_zh: v  服水土； 适应新环境
example: This year he has left for St Louis early to acclimatise himself.

definition_zh: vt  不顾， 不理， 忽视
definition_en: to deliberately pay no attention to something that you have been told or that you know about
example: You can’t ignore the fact that many criminals never go to prison.

definition_zh: n  半球； 大脑半球
definition_en: a half of the Earth, especially one of the halves above and below the  equator
example: the Northern hemisphere

definition_zh: adj  核能的， 原子能的
definition_en: relating to or involving the nucleus (= central part ) of an atom, or the energy produced when the nucleus of an atom is either split or joined with the nucleus of another atom
example: France’s reliance on nuclear energy

definition_zh: v  鼓掌， 喝彩； 称赞
definition_en: to hit your open hands together, to show that you have enjoyed a play, concert, speaker etc
example: The audience applauded loudly.

definition_zh: adj  月的； 月亮的
definition_en: relating to the Moon or to travel to the Moon
example: studies of the lunar surface

definition_zh: v  放松
definition_en: to rest or do something that is enjoyable, especially after you have been working
example: I just want to sit down and relax.

definition_zh: n 接力赛；中继设备['riːleI;ri'leI]; vt  传送； 转播
definition_en: a relay race; if radio or television signals are relayed, they are received and sent, especially so that they can be heard on the radio or seen on television
example: the 100 metres relay

definition_zh: n  房屋， 家， 住所
definition_en: someone’s home – sometimes used humorously
example: Welcome to my humble abode.

definition_zh: n  允许， 准许
definition_en: If someone who has authority over you gives you permission to do something, they say that they will allow you to do it
example: He asked permission to leave the room.

definition_zh: adv  统计上地
example: But until now, no trials have been statistically powered to address effects on survival.

definition_zh: adj  宏伟的； 大的
definition_en: big and very impressive
example: a grand country house

definition_zh: v  扩散， 弥漫； 渗透， 渗入
definition_en: if liquid, gas etc permeates something, it enters it and spreads through every part of it
example: The smell of diesel oil permeated the air.

definition_zh: vt 安排；列入，收进; n  时刻表； 清单
definition_en: to plan that something will happen at a particular time; a list that shows the times that buses, trains etc leave or arrive at a particular place
example: We will not cancel your holiday less than eight weeks before the scheduled departure date .

definition_zh: n  恶化； 堕落
example: Underneath the siding is a "splash base" to further prevent deterioration.

definition_zh: v  解释， 说明； 口译， 翻译
definition_en: to translate spoken words from one language into another
example: They spoke good Spanish, and promised to interpret for me.

definition_zh: n  宇宙， 天地万物； 世界； 领域
definition_en: all space, including all the stars and planets
example: Einstein's equations showed the universe to be expanding.

definition_zh: n  毯子； 厚的覆盖物
definition_en: a cover for a bed, usually made of wool
example: The mud disappeared under a blanket of snow.

definition_zh: v  同意， 承认； 给予， 授予; n 授予物；补助金，助学金，津贴；授权
definition_en: to give someone something or allow them to have something that they have asked for; an amount of money given to someone, especially by the government, for a particular purpose
example: Britain could grant Spain’s request .

definition_zh: n  时事通讯， 业务通讯
definition_en: a short written report of news about a club, organization etc that is sent regularly to people who are interested
example: The society publishes a newsletter twice a year.

definition_zh: adj  能传导电的， 导电的
definition_en: able to conduct electricity, heat etc
example: Copper is a very conductive metal.

definition_zh: n  折扣; vt  给…打折； 漠视， 低估
definition_en: a reduction in the usual price of something; If a shop or company discounts an amount or percentage from something that they are selling, they take the amount or percentage off the usual price
example: They are often available at a discount.

definition_zh: vt 建造；构思，构筑；创立['kɒnstrʌkt]; n  构想， 观念
definition_en: to build something such as a house, bridge, road etc; an idea formed by combining several pieces of information or knowledge
example: There are plans to construct a new road bridge across the river.

definition_zh: v  改变信仰， 皈依； 转变， 转化； 改装
definition_en: to change into a different form, or change into something that can be used for a different purpose or in a different way
example: The signal will be converted into digital code.

definition_zh: n&v  尝试， 试图； 努力
definition_en: an act of trying to do something, especially something difficult
example: Weather conditions prevented them from attempting the jump.

definition_zh: n  分开， 分隔； 分配； 分歧； 除； 部门， 科， 司
definition_en: the act of separating something into two or more different parts, or the way these parts are separated or shared
example: the traditional division of labour (= the way that particular tasks are shared ) between husband and wife

definition_zh: n  航空， 航海， 航行； 导航， 领航
definition_en: the science or job of planning which way you need to go when you are travelling from one place to another
example: compasses and other instruments of navigation

definition_zh: adj  不新鲜的； 陈腐的
definition_en: bread or cake that is stale is no longer fresh or good to eat
example: French bread goes stale (= becomes stale ) very quickly.

definition_zh: n 平衡；余额; vt  平衡
definition_en: a state in which all your weight is evenly spread so that you do not fall; to be in or get into a steady position, without falling to one side or the other, or to put something into this position
example: I lost my balance and fell on my face.

definition_zh: n  指纹， 手印
definition_en: a mark made by the pattern of lines at the end of a person’s finger, which is used by the police to find out who has committed a crime
example: His fingerprints were all over the gun.

definition_zh: v 投掷；颠簸；为…定音高; n  沥青； 场地； 程度； 最高点； 音高
definition_en: if a ship or an aircraft pitches, it moves up and down in an uncontrolled way with the movement of the water or air; a marked out area of ground on which a sport is played
example: She crumpled up the page and pitched it into the fire.

definition_zh: conj  然而， 但是
definition_en: used to say that although something is true of one thing, it is not true of another
example: The old system was fairly complicated whereas the new system is really very simple.

definition_zh: n  洪流； 爆发； 连发
definition_en: A torrent is a lot of water falling or flowing rapidly or violently
example: Torrents of water gushed into the reservoir.

definition_zh: adj  电的， 电学的， 有关电的
definition_en: relating to electricity
example: The fire was caused by an electrical fault.

definition_zh: n  塑像
definition_en: an image of a person or animal that is made in solid material such as stone or metal and is usually large
example: Churchill’s statue stands outside the parliament building.

definition_zh: n  货摊； 小隔间
definition_en: a table or a small shop with an open front, especially outdoors, where goods are sold
example: a market stall

definition_zh: n  分类； 级别
definition_en: a process in which you put something into the group or class it belongs to
example: the classification of wines according to quality

definition_zh: v  酿造； 冲泡； 酝酿
definition_en: to make beer
example: Every beer on the menu was brewed locally.

definition_zh: n 咖喱，
definition_en: a type of food from India, consisting of meat or vegetables in a spicy sauce
example: chicken curry

definition_zh: n  声称， 自命； 自负， 自命不凡
definition_en: an attempt to seem more important, more intelligent, or of a higher class than you really are
example: the humbleness and lack of pretension of Jordan’s cafe

definition_zh: n 威胁；危险的人; vt  威胁到， 危及
definition_en: something or someone that is dangerous; to threaten
example: the growing menace of oil pollution at sea

definition_zh: v  偏斜， 转向
definition_en: if someone or something deflects something that is moving, or if it deflects, it turns in a different direction
example: He deflected the blow with his forearm.

definition_zh: adj  有道德的， 品性好的， 品德高的； 自命清高的； 贞洁的
definition_en: behaving in a very honest and moral way
example: a virtuous man

definition_zh: adj 无效的，作废的；无可靠根据的，站不住脚的；有病的，伤残的['ɪnvælɪd]; n  病弱者， 残疾者
definition_en: a contract, ticket, claim etc that is invalid is not legally or officially acceptable; someone who cannot look after themselves because of illness, old age, or injury
example: Without the right date stamped on it, your ticket will be invalid.

definition_zh: v  同步发生， 同速进行
adj  合法的， 法定的； 正当的， 合情合理的; vt  使合法
definition_en: fair or reasonable
example: That’s a perfectly legitimate question.

definition_zh: n  间隔， 幕间休息； 间距
definition_en: the period of time between two events, activities etc
example: He left the room, returning after a short interval with a message.

definition_zh: adj 世界性的，全球的; n  世界主义者； 四海为家者
definition_en: a cosmopolitan place has people from many different parts of the world – use this to show approval
example: a vibrant, cosmopolitan city

definition_zh: vt  评价， 估价
definition_en: to judge how good, useful, or successful something is
example: You should be able to evaluate your own work.

definition_zh: n  极小量； 螨虫
definition_en: a very small creature that lives in plants,  carpet s  etc
example: ...an itching skin disorder caused by parasitic mites.

definition_zh: adj  歪斜的; vt 使偏；曲解
definition_en: if something skews the results of a test etc, it affects them, making them incorrect
example: However there is some notable right skew.

definition_zh: n  地位； 身份； 状况
definition_en: the official legal position or condition of a person, group, country etc
example: These documents have no legal status in Britain.

definition_zh: adj  语义的
definition_en: relating to the meanings of words
example: He did not want to enter into a semantic debate.

definition_zh: adj  心烦的； 不适的; vt 使苦恼；搅乱；推翻，颠倒[ˌʌp's3t]
definition_en: unhappy and worried because something unpleasant or disappointing has happened; to change a plan or situation in a way that causes problems
example: You’re not still upset with me, are you?

definition_zh: v  打点于； 散布于， 点缀; n 点，小圆点
definition_en: to put a very small amount of something on a surface, especially in several places; a small round mark or spot
example: Poppies dotted the field.

definition_zh: v  撇去； 掠过， 擦过； 浏览， 略读
definition_en: to remove something from the surface of a liquid, especially floating fat, solids, or oil
example: Julie skimmed the sports page.

definition_zh: v 跺，重踏；在…上盖；
definition_en: to put your foot down onto the ground loudly and with a lot of force
example: The audience stamped and shouted.

definition_zh: v  跳， 蹦； 漏过； 逃学
definition_en: to not read, mention, or deal with something that would normally come or happen next
example: I decided to skip the first chapter.

definition_zh: v  散布， 传播
definition_en: to spread information or ideas to as many people as possible
example: Her findings have been widely disseminated .

definition_zh: n  大批离去， 成群外出
definition_en: If there is an exodus of people from a place, a lot of people leave that place at the same time
example: The medical system is facing collapse because of an exodus of doctors.

definition_zh: n&v  提及， 说起
definition_en: to talk or write about something or someone, usually quickly and without saying very much or giving details
example: Was my name mentioned at all?

definition_zh: v  流出; n 溪流；一股
definition_en: to flow quickly and in great amounts; a long and almost continuous series of events, people, objects etc
example: Tears streamed down their faces.

definition_zh: n  洗衣房； 待洗衣服
definition_en: clothes, sheets etc that need to be washed or have just been washed
example: She did the laundry (= washed the clothes etc ) and hung it out to dry.

definition_zh: n 不满，积怨，
definition_en: If you have or bear a grudge against someone, you have unfriendly feelings toward them because of something they did in the past
example: He appears to have a grudge against certain players.

definition_zh: v  积累； 堆积
definition_en: to gradually get more and more money, possessions, knowledge etc over a period of time
example: It is unjust that a privileged few should continue to accumulate wealth.

definition_zh: n  罪行； 犯罪
definition_en: illegal activities in general
example: We moved here because there was very little crime.

definition_zh: vt  围绕； 包围
definition_en: to be all around someone or something on every side
example: He glared at the people who surrounded the tent.

definition_zh: n  空闲时间； 悠闲
definition_en: time when you are not working or studying and can relax and do things you enjoy
example: Most people now enjoy shorter working hours and more leisure time.

definition_zh: n  人行道； 地面， 路面
definition_en: a hard level surface or path at the side of a road for people to walk on
example: A small group of journalists waited on the pavement outside her house.

definition_zh: adj  未被发现的
definition_en: if something, especially something bad, goes undetected, no one notices it
example: The thieves escaped undetected through a basement window.

definition_zh: n  不规则， 无规律
example: Its complexity degree can be quantitatively evaluated using fractal dimension that can describe geometrical irregularity.

definition_zh: v  吸收； 使同化
definition_en: if people assimilate, or are assimilated into a country or group, they become part of that group and are accepted by the people in that group
example: It will take time to assimilate all these facts.

definition_zh: adj  道德的； 伦理的
definition_en: relating to principles of what is right and wrong
example: The president must have the highest ethical standards.

definition_zh: n  耕种， 耕作； 培养， 教育
definition_en: the preparation and use of land for growing crops
example: soil cultivation

definition_zh: n  吸毒成瘾的人， 有瘾的人， 对…入迷的人
definition_en: someone who is unable to stop taking drugs
example: He's only 24 years old and a drug addict.

definition_zh: n  令人惊奇的； 奇特的， 非凡的； 奇迹般的， 不可思议的； 绝妙的
n  铝
definition_en: a silver-white metal that is very light and is used to make cans, cooking pans, window frames etc. It is a chemical  element  : symbol Al
example: recycled aluminium cans

definition_zh: adj  值得信赖的， 可靠的
definition_en: able to be trusted and depended on
example: He is a trustworthy and level-headed leader.

definition_zh: adj  可比较的， 类似的； 比得上的
definition_en: similar to something else in size, number, quality etc, so that you can make a comparison
example: A car of comparable size would cost far more abroad.

definition_zh: n  自尊； 自负
definition_en: the feeling of being satisfied with your own abilities, and that you deserve to be liked or respected
example: students’ sense of self-esteem

definition_zh: adj  …度空间的， …维的
example: If the dimensional shift in either direction is greater than our previously set threshold, increment the total deviation by the amount moved in both directions.

definition_zh: adv  推测起来， 大概
definition_en: used to say that you think something is probably true
example: It’s raining, which presumably means that your football match will be cancelled.

definition_zh: adj  受…支配的， 取决于…的； 易遭受…的; n 题目；学科；主语['sʌbdʒ3kt]
definition_en: To be subject to something means to be affected by it or to be likely to be affected by it; the thing you are talking about or considering in a conversation, discussion, book, film etc
example: Prices may be subject to alteration.

definition_zh: n  奖金， 红利； 好处
definition_en: money added to someone’s wages, especially as a reward for good work
example: Long-term savers qualify for a cash bonus.

definition_zh: n  识别； 认出； 承认， 确认， 认可； 赏识， 表彰
definition_en: the act of realizing and accepting that something is true or important
example: He has achieved recognition and respect as a scientist.

definition_zh: n  走廊， 通道； 侧廊
definition_en: a long passage between rows of seats in a church, plane, theatre etc, or between rows of shelves in a shop
example: Would you like a window seat or an aisle seat (=  seat next to the aisle  ) ?

definition_zh: n 安排；
definition_en: plans and preparations that you must make so that something can happen
example: Have you made all your travel arrangements?

definition_zh: n  收入； 税收
definition_en: money that a business or organization receives over a period of time, especially from selling goods or services
example: advertising revenue

definition_zh: v 剥夺；夺去，使空无一物；拆卸，拆开; n  带状物； 条纹； 狭长地带， 带状水域
definition_en: to remove something that is covering the surface of something else; a long narrow piece of paper, cloth etc
example: Strip the beds and wash the sheets.

definition_zh: n  垄断， 专卖； 垄断商品， 专卖商品
definition_en: if a company or government has a monopoly of a business or political activity, it has complete control of it so that other organizations cannot compete with it
example: a monopoly in copper trading

definition_zh: adj  连续的， 不断的
definition_en: continuing to happen or exist without stopping
example: continuous economic growth

definition_zh: adj  两种语言的
definition_en: written or spoken in two languages
example: a bilingual dictionary

definition_zh: n  住宅， 住处； 居住
definition_en: a house, especially a large or official one
example: the ambassador’s official residence

definition_zh: n  氧， 氧气
definition_en: a gas that has no colour or smell, is present in air, and is necessary for most animals and plants to live. It is a chemical element: symbol O
example: The human brain needs to be without oxygen for only four minutes before permanent damage occurs.

definition_zh: adj  加强的； 密集的
definition_en: involving a lot of activity, effort, or careful attention in a short period of time
example: a one-week intensive course in English

definition_zh: vt  改变； 变动
definition_en: to change, or to make someone or something change
example: Her face hadn’t altered much over the years.

definition_zh: v  联合， 结合
definition_en: if you combine two or more different things, or if they combine, they begin to exist or work together
example: Diets are most effective when combined with exercise.

definition_zh: adj 理想的，完美的；理想主义的；空想的；唯心的; n  理想； 理想的东西
definition_en: the best or most suitable that something could possibly be; a principle about what is morally right or a perfect standard that you hope to achieve
example: advice on how to reach your ideal weight

definition_zh: n  喜剧； 喜剧性
definition_en: entertainment that is intended to make people laugh
example: a career in stand-up comedy

definition_zh: n  扫帚
definition_en: a large brush with a long handle, used for sweeping floors
example: Deer brought the broom.

definition_zh: n  设备， 装备
definition_en: the tools, machines etc that you need to do a particular job or activity
example: a shop selling camping equipment

definition_zh: n 压力，压强；强制，压迫; vt  对…施加压力； 迫使， 说服
definition_en: an attempt to persuade someone by using influence, arguments, or threats; to try to make someone do something by making them feel it is their duty to do it
example: They are putting pressure on people to vote yes.

definition_zh: v 沉思；孵蛋; n  一窝
definition_en: to keep thinking about something that you are worried or upset about; a family of young birds all born at the same time
example: Don’t sit at home brooding all day.

definition_zh: n  有文化； 有教养； 读写能力
definition_en: the state of being able to read and write
example: a new adult literacy campaign

definition_zh: v 喂养，为…提供食物；吃，以…为食; n  饲料
definition_en: to give someone information or ideas over a period time; food for animals
example: We brought along pieces of old bread and fed the birds.

definition_zh: v  起源， 发生； 首创， 创造
definition_en: to come from a particular place or start in a particular situation
example: How did the plan originate?

definition_zh: n 罗盘，罗盘仪；界限，范围；
definition_en: an instrument that shows directions and has a needle that always points north
example: a map and compass

definition_zh: n  解毒药； 矫正方法
definition_en: a substance that stops the effects of a poison
example: When he returned, he noticed their sickness and prepared an antidote.

definition_zh: n  背景； 经历； 后台； 不重要或不引人注目的地方
definition_en: someone’s family, education, previous work etc
example: Students with a background in chemistry will probably find the course easier.

definition_zh: n  宪法， 章程； 体质， 体格； 组成， 形成
definition_en: a set of basic laws and principles that a country or organization is governed by
example: The right to speak freely is written into the Constitution of the United States.

definition_zh: v 混合，混杂; n  混合物； 混合， 交融
definition_en: to thoroughly mix together soft or liquid substances to form a single smooth substance; a product such as tea, tobacco, or  whisky  that is a mixture of several different types
example: Blend the sugar, eggs, and flour.

definition_zh: v  想出， 构想， 设想； 怀孕
definition_en: to imagine a particular situation or to think about something in a particular way
example: Scientists first conceived the idea of the atomic bomb in the 1930’s.

definition_zh: vt 染污；给…着色; n  污点
definition_en: to accidentally make a mark on something, especially one that cannot be removed, or to be marked in this way; a mark that is difficult to remove, especially one made by a liquid such as blood, coffee, or ink
example: Be careful you don’t stain the carpet.

definition_zh: adj 身体的，肉体的；物理的，物理学的；物质的，有形的; n  体检
definition_en: related to someone’s body rather than their mind or emotions; a thorough examination of someone’s body by a doctor, in order to discover whether they are healthy or have any illnesses or medical problems
example: She was in constant physical pain.

definition_zh: n  零售业
definition_en: the business of selling goods to customers in shops
example: There may be many job losses in retailing.

definition_zh: vt  实现， 完成； 满足
definition_en: if you fulfil a hope, wish, or aim, you achieve the thing that you hoped for, wished for etc
example: Visiting Disneyland has fulfilled a boyhood dream .

definition_zh: n  橘子； 达官贵人， 官僚； [M-] 普通话
definition_en: a kind of small orange with skin that is easy to remove

definition_zh: n 结构；构造；建筑物; vt  系统安排； 精心组织
definition_en: the way in which the parts of something are connected with each other and form a whole, or the thing that these parts make up; to arrange the different parts of something into a pattern or system in which each part is connected to the others
example: challenges to the existing power structure

definition_zh: n 桩；火刑柱；利害关系；股份；
definition_en: if you have a stake in a business, you have  invested  money in it
example: Young people don’t feel they have a stake in the country’s future.

definition_zh: n  强烈， 剧烈； 强度
definition_en: the quality of being felt very strongly or having a strong effect
example: The intensity of the hurricane was frightening.

definition_zh: v  目击； 为…作证; n 证据；目击者
definition_en: used to introduce an example that proves something you have just mentioned; someone who sees a crime or an accident and can describe what happened
example: Bad economic times can result in political dictatorships. Witness Germany in the 1930s.

definition_zh: adj  到期的， 预期的； 应有的， 应得的； 正当的
definition_en: expected to happen or arrive at a particular time
example: Any money due you will be sent by cheque through the post.

definition_zh: vi  遵守， 服从； 适应， 顺应； 相一致， 相符合
definition_en: to obey a law, rule etc
example: the pressure on schoolchildren to conform

definition_zh: n  循环， 流通； 流传， 传播； 发行， 发行量
definition_en: the movement of blood around your body
example: Exercise improves the circulation.

definition_zh: adj  真品的， 真迹的； 真正的， 真实的， 逼真的
definition_en: a painting, document, book etc that is authentic has been proved to be by a particular person
example: an authentic work by Picasso

definition_zh: n  偶然性， 可能性； 意外事件； 附带事件
definition_en: an event or situation that might happen in the future, especially one that could cause problems
example: a contingency plan

definition_zh: vt 嘲笑，讥笑
definition_en: If someone taunts you, they say unkind or insulting things to you, especially about your weaknesses or failures
example: A gang taunted a disabled man.

definition_zh: v  混入， 混杂
definition_en: to mix together, or mix things together（
example: Why did another universe intermix itself with the universe of which Earth is a part?

definition_zh: v  通知； 向…报告
definition_en: to officially tell someone about something or give them information
example: They decided to inform the police.

definition_zh: vi  依靠， 依赖； 信赖， 相信； 决定于， 视…而定
definition_en: If you say that one thing depends on another, you mean that the first thing will be affected or determined by the second
example: The cooking time needed depends on the size of the potato.

definition_zh: n  缺乏， 短缺
definition_en: a situation in which there are very few of something that people want or need
example: EXCEPT as Pearl, in the dearth of human playmates, was thrown more upon the visionary throng which she created.

definition_zh: v  把…交托给， 提交； 犯， 干
definition_en: to do something wrong or illegal
example: Women commit fewer crimes than men.

definition_zh: adj  经验主义的， 以经验为依据的
definition_en: based on scientific testing or practical experience, not on ideas
example: {"COLLOINEXA":["empirical evidence"]}

definition_zh: vt  超越， 胜过
definition_en: to be better or more successful than someone else at doing something
example: When it comes to speed of response, a small firm can outdo a big company.

definition_zh: n  悲哀； 伤心事
definition_en: a feeling of great sadness, usually because someone has died or because something terrible has happened to you
example: the family’s joys and sorrows

definition_zh: vt 控制，抑制，约束; n  路缘， 镶边石； 马勒； 起限制作用的事物
definition_en: to control or limit something in order to prevent it from having a harmful effect; the raised edge of a road, between where people can walk and cars can drive
example: measures to curb the spread of the virus

definition_zh: v 碰撞；冲，闯；倒下，坠落；发出撞击声；垮台，破产; adj  速成的; n 碰撞；坠落，坠毁；破裂声，撞击声
definition_en: to have an accident in a car, plane etc by violently hitting something else; a sudden loud noise made by something falling, breaking etc
example: The jet crashed after take-off.

definition_zh: n  降雨， 降水； 仓促， 急躁； 【化】 沉淀作用
definition_en: rain, snow etc that falls on the ground, or the amount of rain, snow etc that falls

definition_zh: n  性格， 品质； 性质， 特性； 人物， 角色； 符号， 字
definition_en: the particular combination of qualities that makes someone a particular type of person
example: He has a cheerful but quiet character.

definition_zh: adj  磁的， 有磁性的； 有吸引力的
definition_en: concerning or produced by  magnetism
example: magnetic forces

definition_zh: n  胆固醇
definition_en: a chemical substance found in your blood. Too much cholesterol in your body may cause heart disease.
example: So why would you want to lower your cholesterol?

definition_zh: v  沿轨道运行； 围绕…运动; n 轨道；影响范围，势力范围
definition_en: to travel in a curved path around a much larger object such as the Earth, the Sun etc; the curved path travelled by an object which is moving around another much larger object such as the Earth, the Sun etc
example: The satellite orbits the Earth every 48 hours.

definition_zh: n  乐队； 群， 伙； 带， 箍； 条纹； 波段， 频带; vt  用带绑扎
definition_en: a group of musicians, especially a group that plays popular music
example: The band was playing old Beatles songs.

definition_zh: adj  常驻的， 居住的; n 居民，住户
definition_en: living in a place; someone who lives or stays in a particular place
example: a resident tutor

definition_zh: vt  包含； 由…组成
definition_en: to consist of particular parts, groups etc
example: The house comprises two bedrooms, a kitchen, and a living room.

definition_zh: n  海拔； 身高
definition_en: how tall someone or something is
example: Sam’s about the same height as his sister now.

definition_zh: adj 温和的；适度的['mɒdəreɪt]; v  减轻， 缓和； 使适中
definition_en: not very large or very small, very hot or very cold, very fast or very slow etc; to make something less extreme or violent, or to become less extreme or violent
example: Even moderate amounts of alcohol can be dangerous.

definition_zh: n  听筒； 接收器
definition_en: the part of a telephone that you hold next to your mouth and ear
example: a satellite receiver

definition_zh: n  仓鼠
definition_en: a small animal that looks like a mouse with no tail
example: Without Leaders, we would be spinning in a hamster wheel with no real vision.

definition_zh: n  模块； 模式； 舱； 组件； 单元
definition_en: one of the separate units that a course of study has been divided into. Usually students choose a number of modules to study
example: a module in mathematics

definition_zh: n  购物中心
definition_en: a large area where there are a lot of shops, usually a covered area where cars are not allowed
example: Let’s meet at the mall and go see a movie.

definition_zh: adj  可观的， 大量的； 坚固的， 结实的； 实质的； 大体上的
definition_en: large in amount or number
example: We have the support of a substantial number of parents.

definition_zh: vt  将…处死； 实施， 执行
definition_en: to kill someone, especially legally as a punishment
example: 13 people were summarily executed (= killed without any trial or legal process ) by the guerrillas.

definition_zh: v 使受益；有益于，
definition_en: if you benefit from something, or it benefits you, it gives you an advantage, improves your life, or helps you in some way
example: They are working together to benefit the whole community.

definition_zh: v  把…制成麦芽； 变成麦芽; n 麦芽；麦芽酒
definition_en: grain, usually  barley  , that has been kept in water for a time and then dried. It is used for making beer,  whisky  etc.

definition_zh: n  小房间； 公用电话亭； 售货棚
definition_en: a small partly enclosed place where one person can do something privately, such as use the telephone or vote
example: a voting booth

definition_zh: adj  唯一的， 独一无二的； 极不寻常的， 极好的
definition_en: unusually good and special
example: a unique opportunity to study these rare creatures

definition_zh: vt 使增长，使兴旺；鼓励，促进；为…作宣传，宣扬; n  增加， 提高； 帮助， 激励
definition_en: to increase or improve something and make it more successful; something that gives someone more confidence, or that helps something increase, improve, or become successful
example: The new resort area has boosted tourism.

definition_zh: adj  真挚的， 诚挚的， 真诚的， 诚恳的
definition_en: a feeling, belief, or statement that is sincere is honest and true, and based on what you really feel and believe
example: Please accept my sincere apologies .

definition_zh: v  翻， 倾覆
definition_en: if a boat capsizes, or if you capsize it, it turns over in the water
example: The sea got very rough and the boat capsized.

definition_zh: n  部
definition_en: a government department that is responsible for one of the areas of government work, such as education or health
example: ...the Ministry of Justice.

definition_zh: adj  母亲的； 母系的
definition_en: typical of the way a good mother behaves or feels
example: Annie was wonderfully warm and maternal.

definition_zh: vt  把…开除； 驱逐
definition_en: to officially force someone to leave a school or organization
example: More than five-thousand high school students have been expelled for cheating.

definition_zh: v  掩蔽； 躲避; n 掩蔽，隐蔽；住所；保护
definition_en: to stay in or under a place where you are protected from the weather or from danger; protection from danger or from wind, rain, hot sun etc
example: Collins was arrested for sheltering enemy soldiers.

definition_zh: vt  检查； 调查， 研究； 测验
definition_en: to look at something carefully and thoroughly because you want to find out more about it
example: A team of divers was sent down to examine the wreck.

definition_zh: n 纬度；
definition_en: the distance north or south of the equator(= the imaginary line around the middle of the world ), measured in degrees
example: The birds breed in northern latitudes.

definition_zh: v 扫描；浏览; n  扫描
definition_en: to read something quickly; a medical test in which a special machine produces a picture of something inside your body
example: She scanned through the paper.

definition_zh: n  协会
definition_en: an organization that consists of a group of people who have the same aims, do the same kind of work etc
example: the Association of Master Builders

definition_zh: vt  消灭， 消除； 淘汰
definition_en: to completely get rid of something that is unnecessary or unwanted
example: Our team was eliminated in the first round.

definition_zh: n  垃圾堆； 垃圾填筑地， 废渣埋填地
definition_en: a place where waste is buried under the ground
example: ...the environmental costs of landfill.

definition_zh: vt  增加， 提高， 扩大
definition_en: to increase the value, amount, effectiveness etc of something
example: Any surplus was sold to augment their income.

definition_zh: adj  可辨认的， 可识别的； 可确定的， 可证明是同一的
definition_en: able to be recognized
example: In the corridor were four dirty, ragged bundles, just identifiable as human beings.

definition_zh: n  子宫； 发源地
definition_en: the part of a woman’s or female animal’s body where her baby grows before it is born
example: ...the development of the fetus in the womb.

definition_zh: n  破坏， 毁灭， 摧毁
definition_en: the act or process of destroying something or of being destroyed
example: {"COLLOINEXA":["weapons of mass destruction"]}

definition_zh: adv  极其， 非常; adj 硬的，僵硬的
definition_en: extremely bored, frightened, or worried; if someone or a part of their body is stiff, their muscles hurt and it is difficult for them to move
example: As a child, I was scared stiff of going down to the cellar.

definition_zh: vi  繁荣， 兴旺； 成功
definition_en: if people or businesses prosper, they grow and develop in a successful way, especially by becoming rich or making a large profit
example: Businesses across the state are prospering.

definition_zh: v 打扰，烦扰；烦恼，操心; n  麻烦， 烦扰
definition_en: to make the effort to do something; trouble or difficulty that has been caused by small problems and that usually only continues for a short time
example: Why bother to go abroad, when there are so many nice places here?

definition_zh: n  更新， 恢复； 重新开始； 重建； 复兴； 续期； 重申
definition_en: when an activity, situation, or process begins again after a period when it had stopped
example: Mark’s contract comes up for renewal at the end of this year.

definition_zh: vi 呼吁；起诉，上诉；吸引; n  上诉， 申诉； 呼吁； 感染力， 吸引力
definition_en: to make a serious public request for help, money, information etc; an urgent request for something important
example: She is not happy with the decision and plans to appeal.

definition_zh: adj  心血管的
definition_en: relating to the heart and  blood vessel s (= tubes through which blood flows around your body )
example: cardiovascular disease

definition_zh: vt 淹没；
definition_en: to suddenly cover an area with a lot of water
example: Huge waves swamped the vessel.

definition_zh: n  企业家； 承包人
definition_en: someone who starts a new business or arranges business deals in order to make money, often in a way that involves financial risks
example: How would you define personal growth as an entrepreneur?

definition_zh: n  运输， 运输系统， 运输工具
definition_en: a system or method for carrying passengers or goods from one place to another
example: The city needs to improve its public transportation (= buses, trains etc ) .

definition_zh: vt  献 ； 题献词
definition_en: to say at the beginning of a book or film, or before a piece of music, that it has been written, made, or performed for someone that you love or respect
example: She dedicated her first album to Woody Allen, who she says understands her obsession.

definition_zh: v  出现， 显露， 公开露面； 出场， 问世； 仿佛， 似乎
definition_en: used to say that someone or something seems to have a particular quality or feeling
example: He tried hard to appear calm .

definition_zh: adj  广大的， 广阔的； 广泛的
definition_en: large in size, amount, or degree
example: The house stands in extensive grounds.

definition_zh: adj  居住的， 住宅的； 寄宿的
definition_en: a residential part of a town consists of private houses, with no offices or factories
example: a quiet residential neighbourhood

definition_zh: v  刺， 戳
definition_en: to push a knife into someone or something
example: He was stabbed to death in a fight.

definition_zh: adj  单调的， 无聊的
definition_en: boring because of always being the same
example: a monotonous diet

definition_zh: vt  鼓舞， 激起； 给…以灵感
definition_en: to encourage someone by making them feel confident and eager to do something
example: We need someone who can inspire the team.

definition_zh: vt  给予， 赋予； 传授； 告知， 透露
definition_en: to give a particular quality to something
example: She had information that she couldn’t wait to impart.

definition_zh: n  合作， 协作； 勾结， 通敌
definition_en: when you work together with another person or group to achieve something, especially in science or art
example: The company is building the centre in collaboration with the Institute of Offshore Engineering.

definition_zh: n  官僚主义； 官僚机构
definition_en: a complicated official system that is annoying or confusing because it has a lot of rules, processes etc
example: the reduction of unnecessary bureaucracy

definition_zh: vt  使合法； 赋予合法权利
definition_en: to make something official or legal
example: Acceptance by the UN would effectively legitimize the regime.

definition_zh: n  几何； 几何学
definition_en: the study in  mathematics  of the angles and shapes formed by the relationships of lines, surfaces, and solid objects in space
example: ...the very ordered way in which mathematics and geometry describe nature.

definition_zh: vi&n  滑行， 滑翔
definition_en: to move smoothly and quietly, as if without effort
example: Waiters glide between tightly packed tables bearing trays of pasta.

definition_zh: v 使劳累过分；对…使用过度；滥用; n  过重的工作； 工作过度
definition_en: to work too much or to make someone work too much; too much hard work
example: You’ve been overworking – why don’t you take a week off?

definition_zh: vt  将…奉献给； 致力
definition_en: to use all or most of your time, effort etc in order to do something or help someone
example: He decided to devote the rest of his life to scientific investigation.

definition_zh: v  投资； 投入； 授予， 赋予
definition_en: to buy shares, property, or goods because you hope that the value will increase and you can make a profit
example: I’ve got a few thousand dollars I’m looking to invest.

definition_zh: adj 近似的[ə'prɒksɪm3ɪt]; vt  近似
definition_en: to be close to a particular number
example: For each approximate solution of a parameterized problem, the app also displays the margin of error.

definition_zh: v  放映； 投射， 发射； 突出， 伸出； 设计， 规划; n 计划，方案；课题，项目；工程[prə'dʒekt]
definition_en: to stick out beyond an edge or surface; a carefully planned piece of work to get information about something, to build something, to improve something etc
example: projecting teeth

definition_zh: adj  侧面的， 旁边的
definition_en: relating to the sides of something, or movement to the side
example: The wall is weak and requires lateral support.

definition_zh: n  内地， 腹地， 内陆
definition_en: an area of land that is far from the coast, large rivers, or the places where people live
example: the rural hinterland

definition_zh: vt  使…凸出； 压花
vt  使陷于危险， 危及
definition_en: to put something or someone in danger
example: Tax increases now might imperil economic recovery.

definition_zh: n  直径
definition_en: a straight line from one side of a circle to the other side, passing through the centre of the circle, or the length of this line
example: ...a tube less than a fifth of the diameter of a human hair.

definition_zh: adj  静止的， 不动的， 固定的； 稳定的
definition_en: standing still instead of moving
example: How did you manage to drive into a stationary vehicle?

definition_zh: vi 吠叫; n  犬吠声； 树皮
definition_en: when a dog barks, it makes a short loud sound or series of sounds; the outer covering of a tree
example: Don't let the dogs bark.

definition_zh: v  成环， 成圈； 成环形运动; n 圈环；环状物；环路；循环
definition_en: to move in a circular direction that forms the shape of a loop; a shape like a curve or a circle made by a line curving back towards itself, or a piece of wire, string etc that has this shape
example: The ball looped over the goalkeeper into the back of the net.

definition_zh: adj  赤裸的， 光秃的
definition_en: not covered by clothes
example: a ragged child with bare feet

definition_zh: adj  可怕的； 难以应付的， 难以克服的
definition_en: very powerful or impressive, and often frightening
example: The building is grey, formidable, not at all picturesque.

definition_zh: vi  富于； 充满
definition_en: If things abound, or if a place abounds with things, there are very large numbers of them
example: Stories abound about when he was in charge.

definition_zh: n  手提箱； 衣箱
definition_en: a large case with a handle, used for carrying clothes and possessions when you travel
example: It did not take Andrew long to pack a suitcase.

definition_zh: vt  倒转， 颠倒
definition_en: to put something in the opposite position to the one it was in before, especially by turning it upside down (= the bottom is on the top and the top is on the bottom )
example: Invert the cake onto a serving plate.

definition_zh: n 曲柄，曲轴; vt  用曲柄转动某物
definition_en: a handle on a piece of equipment, that you can turn in order to move something; to make something move by turning a crank
example: He was idly turning a crank on a strange mechanism strapped to his chest.

definition_zh: n  吸盘； 吸根， 根出条
definition_en: a part of an insect or of an animal’s body that it uses to hold on to a surface

definition_zh: n  征服； 战利品
definition_en: the act of getting control of a country by fighting
example: the Norman Conquest (= the conquest of England by the Normans )

definition_zh: n  修改， 改正
definition_en: a small change made in something such as a design, plan, or system
example: They have used the same process for almost 50 years with only minor modifications .

definition_zh: adj  密集的； 浓厚的
definition_en: made of or containing a lot of things or people that are very close together
example: a dense rurally-based population

definition_zh: n  信心； 保证； 保险
definition_en: a promise that something will definitely happen or is definitely true, made especially to make someone less worried
example: Despite my repeated assurances, Rob still looked very nervous.

definition_zh: n  两星期， 十四天
definition_en: two weeks
example: a fortnight’s holiday

definition_zh: vt  使中断； 扰乱
definition_en: to prevent something from continuing in its usual way by causing problems
example: Traffic was disrupted by a hoax bomb.

definition_zh: n  护目镜； 风镜； 游泳镜
definition_en: a pair of  glasses  made of glass or plastic with a rubber or plastic edge that fit against your skin and protect your eyes
example: And protect your eyes from snow and ice glare with dark glasses or goggles.

definition_zh: adj  假设的， 假定的； 爱猜想的
definition_en: based on a situation that is not real, but that might happen
example: The question is purely hypothetical .

definition_zh: vi 谈话，会谈['kɒnvɜːs]; adj 逆向的; n  相反的事物； 反面
definition_en: to have a conversation with someone; the converse of a fact, word, statement etc is the opposite of it
example: Luke sat directly behind the pilot and conversed with him.

definition_zh: adj 古怪的，反常的; n  古怪的人
definition_en: behaving in a way that is unusual and different from most people; someone who behaves in a way that is different from what is usual or socially accepted
example: His eccentric behaviour lost him his job.

definition_zh: n  告别； 欢送会
definition_en: the action of saying goodbye
example: Mourners gathered to bid farewell to the victims of the plane tragedy.

definition_zh: n  用具， 器具
definition_en: a piece of equipment, especially electrical equipment, such as a  cooker  or  washing machine  , used in people’s homes
example: He could also learn to use the vacuum cleaner, the washing machine and other household appliances.

definition_zh: adj 大量的; v  聚集; n 团，众多；【物】质量；
definition_en: involving or intended for a very large number of people; to come together, or to make people or things come together, in a large group; the amount of material in something
example: a mass protest

definition_zh: n  [the R-] 文艺复兴， 文艺复兴时期； 复兴， 新生
definition_en: a new interest in something, especially a particular form of art, music etc, that has not been popular for a long period
example: ...the Renaissance masterpieces in London's galleries.

definition_zh: adj  有毒的， 有害的； 恶毒的， 邪恶的
definition_en: containing poison or producing poison
example: Some mushrooms are extremely poisonous.

definition_zh: adj  可推断的， 推理的
definition_en: of, relating to, or derived from inference
example: Finally, people like to stereotype. This is an inferential process based on limited data.

definition_zh: v  做手势; n 姿势，姿态；手势
definition_en: to move your hand, arm, or head to tell someone something, or show them what you mean; a movement of part of your body, especially your hands or head, to show what you mean or how you feel
example: I gestured toward the boathouse, and he looked inside.

definition_zh: v  承认； 准许…进入； 准许…加入
definition_en: to agree unwillingly that something is true or that someone else is right
example: ‘Okay, so maybe I was a little bit scared,’ Jenny admitted.

definition_zh: vt  推进， 推动； 驱使
definition_en: to move, drive, or push something forward
example: a boat propelled by a small motor

definition_zh: adj  猖獗的， 肆虐的， 泛滥的； 蔓生的， 疯长的
definition_en: if something bad, such as crime or disease, is rampant, there is a lot of it and it is very difficult to control
example: Pickpocketing is rampant in the downtown area.

definition_zh: n 兴趣；利息；利益; vt  使感兴趣
definition_en: if you have an interest in something or someone, you want to know or learn more about them; to make someone want to pay attention to something and find out more about it
example: I watched the first few episodes, but soon lost interest.

definition_zh: adj  器官的； 有机的； 有机体的； 组织的
definition_en: living, or produced by or from living things
example: Adding organic matter such as manure can improve the soil.

definition_zh: v  申请； 应用， 使用； 涉及
definition_en: to make a formal request, usually written, for something such as a job, a place at a university, or permission to do something
example: We need to apply for planning permission to build a garage.

definition_zh: n  行话， 行业术语； 黑话
definition_en: words and expressions used in a particular profession or by a particular group of people, which are difficult for other people to understand – often used to show disapproval
example: Keep it simple and avoid the use of jargon.

definition_zh: adj  不可信的； 难以置信的
definition_en: too strange to be believed, or very difficult to believe
example: It’s incredible that he survived the fall.

definition_zh: adj  太空的； 轻巧的
example: ethereal beauty

definition_zh: n  布局， 安排， 设计
definition_en: the way in which something such as a town, garden, or building is arranged
example: All the flats in the building had the same layout.

definition_zh: v  不同意； 不一致
definition_en: to have or express a different opinion from someone else
example: Barr strongly disagreed with Kronfeld’s statement.

definition_zh: n&v  雪崩
definition_en: a large mass of snow, ice, and rocks that falls down the side of a mountain
example: Two skiers were killed in the avalanche.

definition_zh: n  公式； 配方； 准则
definition_en: a method or set of principles that you use to solve a problem or to make sure that something is successful
example: We’re still searching for a peace formula.

definition_zh: n  贡品； 颂词， 称赞； 礼物
definition_en: something that you say, do, or give in order to express your respect or admiration for someone
example: The players wore black armbands as a tribute to their late teammate.

definition_zh: vt  危及， 危害
definition_en: to put someone or something in danger of being hurt, damaged, or destroyed
example: Smoking during pregnancy endangers your baby’s life.

definition_zh: n 茎；干；词干; vt  止住， 抑止， 堵住， 阻止
definition_en: the long thin part of a plant, from which leaves, flowers, or fruit grow; to stop something from happening, spreading, or developing
example: long-stemmed wine glasses

definition_zh: adj  过分单纯化的， 过分简单化的
definition_en: treating difficult subjects in a way that is too simple
example: This is a very simplistic approach to the problem.

definition_zh: adj  下级的； 次要的; n 下属; vt  使处于次要地位， 把…列在下级； 使服从
definition_en: in a less important position than someone else; someone who has a lower position and less authority than someone else in an organization; to put someone or something in a less important position
example: a subordinate officer

definition_zh: vi  伸向； 倾向; n 倾向；趋势；流行，时尚
definition_en: a general tendency in the way a situation is changing or developing
example: The current trend is towards more part-time employment.

definition_zh: n&vt  正式要求； 征用
definition_en: if someone in authority, especially the army, requisitions a building, vehicle, or food, they officially demand to have it during an  emergency  such as a war
example: The building was requisitioned as a military hospital for the duration of the war.

definition_zh: n  成就， 成绩； 达到， 完成， 实现
definition_en: something important that you succeed in doing by your own efforts
example: His great achievement is to make all the players into a united team.

definition_zh: n  机修工； [-s] 力学， 机械学
definition_en: someone who is skilled at repairing motor vehicles and machinery
example: a garage mechanic

definition_zh: n  行程表； 旅行路线， 旅行计划
definition_en: a plan or list of the places you will visit on a journey
example: The next place on our itinerary was Sedona.

definition_zh: n  革命； 巨变， 大变革
definition_en: a complete change in ways of thinking, methods of working etc
example: the French Revolution of 1789

definition_zh: n  关系， 联系； 亲属， 亲戚
definition_en: a connection between two or more things
example: The price the meat is sold for bears no relation to (= is not connected to ) the price the farmer receives.

definition_zh: adj  可靠的， 可信赖的
definition_en: someone or something that is reliable can be trusted or depended on
example: a birth control method that is cheap and reliable

definition_zh: n  刺激； 激励
definition_en: something that encourages you to work harder, start a new activity etc
example: As an added incentive, there’s a bottle of champagne for the best team.

definition_zh: v  有关联； 讲述， 叙述
definition_en: if two things relate, they are connected in some way
example: I don’t understand how the two ideas relate.

definition_zh: n  受雇的或现有的工作人员总数； 劳动人口
definition_en: The workforce is the total number of people in a country or region who are physically able to do a job and are available for work
example: ...a country where half the workforce is unemployed.

definition_zh: adj 开端的，预备的，初步的
definition_en: happening before something that is more important, often in order to prepare for it
example: the preliminary stages of the competition

definition_zh: n  障碍， 妨碍物， 干扰
definition_en: something that makes it difficult to achieve some­thing
example: The tax puts obstacles in the way of companies trying to develop trade overseas.

definition_zh: adj  易变的， 无常的
definition_en: someone who is fickle is always changing their mind about people or things that they like, so that you cannot depend on them – used to show disapproval
example: an unpredictable and fickle lover

definition_zh: n  运货车
definition_en: a vehicle used especially for carrying goods, which is smaller than a truck and has a roof and usually no windows at the sides
example: a delivery van

definition_zh: n  虫， 蠕虫； 蜗杆， 螺纹
definition_en: a long thin creature with no bones and no legs that lives in soil
example: ...a new computer worm that disables security software.

definition_zh: adj  头晕目眩的， 眩晕的； 人头晕的
definition_en: feeling unable to stand steadily, for example because you are looking down from a high place or because you are ill
example: The heat and the champagne made him feel dizzy .

definition_zh: adj  例外的； 异常的
definition_en: unusually good
example: an exceptional student

definition_zh: v  组成； 构成； 设立， 成立
definition_en: if several people or things constitute something, they are the parts that form it
example: We must redefine what constitutes a family.

definition_zh: n  合法性； 正统性
example: He continues to cite the article as evidence of his legitimacy.

definition_zh: v 交配，配种; n  配偶； 伙伴； 大副
definition_en: if animals mate, they have sex to produce babies; the sexual partner of an animal
example: Rabbits can be mated as early as six months old.

definition_zh: vt  确信； 说服
definition_en: to make someone feel certain that something is true
example: Her arguments didn’t convince everyone, but changes were made.

definition_zh: v 狼吞虎咽，
definition_en: If you gorge on something or gorge yourself on it, you eat lots of it in a very greedy way
example: I could spend each day gorging on chocolate.

definition_zh: n  登记， 注册； 挂号； 登记或注册的项目
definition_en: the act of recording names and details on an official list
example: Student registration (= for a course of study ) starts the first week in September.

definition_zh: n  理论， 原理； 学说； 意见， 看法
definition_en: an idea or set of ideas that is intended to explain something about life or the world, especially an idea that has not yet been proved to be true
example: Freudian theory has had a great influence on psychology.

definition_zh: adj 欧洲大陆的；大陆性的; n  欧洲大陆人
definition_en: relating to a large mass of land
example: the warming-up of continental interiors

definition_zh: adj  内部的； 国内的
definition_en: within a particular country
example: We have no interest in interfering in the internal affairs of other countries.

definition_zh: n  伸展； 延长部分； 电话分机
definition_en: the process of making a road, building etc bigger or longer, or the part that is added
example: a loft extension

definition_zh: n  性别； 性
definition_en: the fact of being male or female
example: people of the same gender

definition_zh: n  签名， 签字； 签署
definition_en: your name written in the way you usually write it, for example at the end of a letter, or on a cheque etc, to show that you have written it
example: Her signature is totally illegible (= cannot be read ) .

definition_zh: n  建筑师， 设计师； 创造者
definition_en: someone whose job is to design buildings
example: Tinoco was one of the architects of the government’s economic reforms.

definition_zh: v 〔因紧张或惊慌而〕反胃; n 〔制造黄油的〕搅乳器
definition_en: if your stomach churns, you feel sick because you are nervous or frightened; a container used for shaking milk in order to make it into butter
example: My stomach was churning on the day of the exam.

definition_zh: n  基础； 地基； 创立
definition_en: the solid layer of  cement  , bricks, stones etc that is put under a building to support it
example: It took the builders three weeks to lay the foundations .

definition_zh: n  占星学； 占星术
definition_en: the study of the positions and movements of the stars and how they might influence people and events
example: Why do people believe in astrology?

definition_zh: n  可变性， 易变性
example: In our world, not only do we have variability, we've got unpredictability.

definition_zh: n  入侵者
definition_en: a soldier or a group of soldiers that enters a country or town by force in order to take control of it
example: Invaders ransacked the town.

definition_zh: v 穿孔，打孔；重击，猛击; n  猛击； 冲床， 穿孔机； 力量， 效力
definition_en: to hit someone or something hard with your  fist (= closed hand ) ; a strong effective way of expressing things that makes people interested
example: He punched me and knocked my teeth out.

definition_zh: adv  当然， 自然； 天生地
definition_en: use this to say that something is normal and not surprising
example: Naturally, you’ll want to discuss this with your wife.

definition_zh: n  柔韧， 有弹性； 柔顺， 温顺； 灵活性； 适应性
definition_en: the ability to change or be changed easily to suit a different situation
example: Employees expect flexibility in the workplace.

definition_zh: n  身份； 特性； 同一性
definition_en: someone’s identity is their name or who they are
example: The identity of the killer is still unknown.

definition_zh: adj  联邦的， 联盟的
definition_en: a federal country or system of government consists of a group of states which control their own affairs, but which are also controlled by a single national government which makes decisions on foreign affairs, defence etc
example: Switzerland is a federal republic.

definition_zh: vt  证明； 证实
definition_en: to state that something is true
example: His statement was verified by several witnesses.

definition_zh: n  远足， 短途旅游； 【物】 偏移， 漂移
definition_en: a short journey arranged so that a group of people can visit a place, especially while they are on holiday
example: a shopping excursion

definition_zh: adj  势利的， 谄上欺下的； 自命不凡的
definition_en: behaving in a way that shows you think you are better than other people because you are from a higher social class or know more than they do
example: Her family seems snobbish.

definition_zh: vt  开始， 开创； 为…举行就职典礼； 为…举行开幕式； 为…举行落成仪式
definition_en: to hold an official ceremony when someone starts doing an important job in government
example: The Turner Prize was inaugurated in 1984.

definition_zh: n 奖赏；报酬; vt  酬谢； 奖励
definition_en: something that you get because you have done something good or helpful or have worked hard; to give something to someone because they have done something good or helpful or have worked for it
example: The school has a system of rewards and punishments to encourage good behaviour.

definition_zh: v  包括， 包含； 计入
definition_en: if one thing includes another, the second thing is part of the first
example: Does the price include postage?

definition_zh: n  曲棍球； 冰球
definition_en: a game played on grass by two teams of 11 players, with sticks and a ball
example: ...a new hockey arena.

definition_zh: v 吞咽；忍受; n  燕子
definition_en: to make food or drink go down your throat and towards your stomach; a small black and white bird that comes to northern countries in the summer
example: He swallowed the last of his coffee and asked for the bill.

definition_zh: n  利益； 特权
example: In so many countries that I covered in Southeast Asia it was a given that the elites would take over public land as a kind of perquisite of power.

definition_zh: n  定量； 限额
n  合并； 巩固
definition_en: the act of consolidating or state of being consolidated
example: The consolidation of the beautifier of life, the society.

definition_zh: vt 培养；滋养; n  营养品
definition_en: to help a plan, idea, feeling etc to develop
example: European union is an ideal that has been nurtured since the post-war years.

definition_zh: adj  破坏的
definition_en: causing damage to people or things
example: the destructive power of modern weapons

definition_zh: adj  适宜耕种的， 可耕种的
vi 退化[dɪ'dʒ3nərət]; adj  退化的
definition_en: to become worse; morally unacceptable
example: Inactivity can make your joints stiff, and the bones may begin to degenerate.

definition_zh: v  扣紧； 变形； 弯曲; n 皮带扣环
definition_en: to become bent or curved because of heat or pressure, or to make something bend or curve in this way; a piece of metal used for fastening the two ends of a belt, for fastening a shoe, bag etc, or for decoration
example: The steel pillars began to buckle.

definition_zh: adj  草率的， 匆忙的
definition_en: done in a hurry, especially with bad results
example: He soon regretted his hasty decision.

definition_zh: vt 审查，检查; n  检查员
definition_en: to examine books, films, letters etc to remove anything that is considered offensive, morally harmful, or politically dangerous etc; someone whose job is to examine books, films, letters etc and remove anything considered to be offensive, morally harmful, or politically dangerous
example: The information given to the press was carefully censored by the Ministry of Defence.

definition_zh: v&n  搅动； 摇动； 激动
definition_en: If you stir a liquid or other substance, you move it around or mix it in a container using something such as a spoon
example: Stir the soup for a few seconds.

definition_zh: n  工艺， 手艺； 船； 航空器
definition_en: a job or activity in which you make things with your hands, and that you usually need skill to do
example: traditional rural crafts

definition_zh: n  存在； 生活
definition_en: the state of existing
example: Scientists have many theories about how the universe first came into existence (= started to exist ).

definition_zh: adj  迁徙的， 移居的； 流动的， 游牧的
definition_en: involved in or relating to migration
example: migratory birds

definition_zh: n  入门， 门槛； 界限； 开端， 起点
definition_en: the entrance to a room or building, or the area of floor or ground at the entrance
example: She opened the door and stepped across the threshold.

definition_zh: vt  打开， 解开； 除去包装
definition_en: to remove the paper, plastic etc that is around something
example: Brigitte was unwrapping her birthday presents.

definition_zh: adj  处于被告地位的； 为自己辩护的; n 被告
definition_en: the person in a court of law who has been  accused  of doing something illegal
example: We find the defendant not guilty.

definition_zh: adj  粗的； 粗糙的； 粗劣的
definition_en: having a rough surface that feels slightly hard
example: a jacket of coarse wool

definition_zh: n  小巷； 胡同
definition_en: a narrow street between or behind buildings, not usually used by cars
example: The alley led to the railway bridge.

definition_zh: v  夸大， 夸张
definition_en: to make something seem better, larger, worse etc than it really is
example: I couldn’t sleep for three days – I’m not exaggerating.

definition_zh: vt  发现， 找到； 发觉
definition_en: to find someone or something, either by accident or because you were looking for them
example: The body was discovered in a field.

definition_zh: vt 审查，仔细检查; n  兽医
definition_en: to check someone’s past activities, relationships etc in order to make sure that person is suitable for a particular job, especially an important one; someone who is trained to give medical care and treatment to sick animals
example: All candidates are carefully vetted by Central Office.

definition_zh: vt  抵消， 补偿， 弥补
definition_en: if the cost or amount of something offsets another cost or amount, the two things have an opposite effect so that the situation remains the same
example: Cuts in food prices will be offset by direct payments to farmers.

definition_zh: n  小瀑布
definition_en: a small steep  waterfall  that is one of several together
example: Failure can cause a system imbalance, resulting in a pressure cascade malfunction with a resultant airflow reversal.

definition_zh: vt 捕获，俘获；夺取或赢得; n  捕获； 战利品
definition_en: to catch a person and keep them as a prisoner; when you catch someone in order to make them a prisoner
example: Government troops have succeeded in capturing the rebel leader.

definition_zh: adj  有读写能力的； 有文化的； 博学的
definition_en: able to read and write
