example: Over one-quarter of the adult population are not fully literate.

definition_zh: n  资格， 合格； 技能； 限定， 条件； 合格证
definition_en: if you have a qualification, you have passed an examination or course to show you have a particular level of skill or knowledge in a subject
example: He left school without any qualifications.

definition_zh: n  一系列， 一连串； 顺序， 次序
definition_en: the order that something happens or exists in, or the order it is supposed to happen or exist in
example: Be careful to perform the actions in the correct sequence .

definition_zh: n  燃烧； 燃烧过程
definition_en: the process of burning
example: The energy is released by combustion on the application of a match.

definition_zh: n  供应， 供应品； 准备， 预备； 条款， 规定； 给养， 口粮
definition_en: when you provide something that someone needs now or in the future
example: He made provisions for his wife and his children in his will.

definition_zh: v 招募，招收；复原，恢复；补充; n  新兵， 新成员， 新会员
definition_en: to find new people to work in a company, join an organization, do a job etc; someone who has just joined the army, navy, or  air force
example: We’re having difficulty recruiting enough qualified staff.

definition_zh: v  开始， 着手
definition_en: to begin or to start something
example: Work will commence on the new building immediately.

definition_zh: n  结构， 配置； 轮廓， 外形
definition_en: the combination of equipment needed to run a computer system
example: Prices range from $119 to $199, depending on the particular configuration.

definition_zh: n  大块； 相当大的部分
definition_en: a large thick piece of something that does not have an even shape
example: ice chunks

definition_zh: n  平台， 站台； 纲领
definition_en: the raised place beside a railway track where you get on and off a train in a station
example: The Edinburgh train will depart from platform six.

definition_zh: n 诽谤，中伤；诽谤性文字; vt  诽谤， 中伤， 诬蔑
definition_en: when someone writes or prints untrue statements about someone so that other people could have a bad opinion of them; to write or print a libel against someone
example: restrictions on press freedom, such as libel laws

definition_zh: n  出处， 起源
definition_en: the place where something originally came from
example: The provenance of the paintings is unknown.

definition_zh: adj  灭绝的； 废弃的
definition_en: an extinct type of animal or plant does not exist any more
example: Dinosaurs have been extinct for millions of years.

definition_zh: n  车轴， 轮轴
definition_en: the bar connecting two wheels on a car or other vehicle
example: Remove the upper suspension arm nut and bolt at the axle bracket.

definition_zh: v  降级； 堕落； 降解； 退化
definition_en: if a substance, chemical etc degrades, or if something degrades it, it changes to a simpler form

definition_zh: n  生物多样性
definition_en: the variety of plants and animals in a particular place
example: the biodiversity of the rainforest

definition_zh: adj  不透明的； 晦涩的， 难懂的
definition_en: opaque glass or liquid is difficult to see through and often thick
example: a shower with an opaque glass door

definition_zh: n  优点， 优势
definition_en: something that helps you to be more successful than others, or the state of having this
example: Younger workers tend to be at an advantage (=  have an advantage  ) when applying for jobs.

definition_zh: adj 助理的；
definition_en: someone whose job is just below the level of manager etc
example: Whose assistant should I work as?

definition_zh: vt 对…估计过高[ˌəuvər'estImət]; n  过高的估计
definition_en: to think something is better, more important etc than it really is; a calculation, judgement, or guess that is too large
example: He tends to overestimate his own abilities.

definition_zh: adj  湿的， 潮湿的， 湿润的， 多潮气的
definition_en: if the weather is humid, you feel uncomfortable because the air is very wet and usually hot
example: Tokyo is extremely humid in mid-summer.

definition_zh: n  争论， 辩论， 论战
definition_en: a serious argument about something that involves many people and continues for a long time
example: a political controversy

definition_zh: adj  义务的； 必须做的； 强制性的； 必修的
definition_en: something that is compulsory must be done because it is the law or because someone in authority orders you to
example: the threat of compulsory redundancies

definition_zh: v  预期， 预料， 期望； 先于…行动
definition_en: to expect that something will happen and be ready for it
example: Sales are better than anticipated.

definition_zh: v  分开， 分离； 分遣
definition_en: If you detach one thing from another that it is attached to, you remove it. If one thing detaches from another, it becomes separated from it.
example: Detach the white part of the application form and keep it for reference only.

definition_zh: v  砍； 大幅度削减; n 砍痕，伤痕；斜线号
definition_en: to cut or try to cut something violently with a knife, sword etc; a line (/) used in writing to separate words, numbers, or letters
example: Someone had slashed the tires.

definition_zh: vt  勾引， 引诱； 诱使， 唆使
definition_en: to persuade someone to have sex with you, especially in a way that is attractive and not too direct
example: The head lecturer was sacked for seducing female students.

definition_zh: n 风景; vt  对…作景观美化， 美化
definition_en: an area of countryside or land of a particular type, used especially when talking about its appearance; to make a park, garden etc look attractive and interesting by changing its design, and by planting trees and bushes etc
example: the beauty of the New England landscape

definition_zh: adj  临时的， 暂时的
definition_en: continuing for only a limited period of time
example: temporary pain relief

definition_zh: n  栖息地， 住处
definition_en: the natural home of a plant or animal
example: watching monkeys in their natural habitat

definition_zh: adj  绝望的； 拼命的， 不顾一切的； 极度渴望的； 危急的
definition_en: willing to do anything to change a very bad situation, and not caring about danger
example: I had no money left and was desperate.

definition_zh: adj  糟糕的
definition_en: very bad or unpleasant
example: The weather was awful.

definition_zh: adj  方便的； 手边的； 手巧的
definition_en: near and easy to reach
example: I always keep my gun handy just in case.

definition_zh: n  树枝； 分部， 分科
definition_en: a part of a tree that grows out from the  trunk (=  main stem  )  and that has leaves, fruit, or smaller branches growing from it
example: After the storm, the ground was littered with twigs and branches.

definition_zh: v  敦促， 力劝； 鼓励； 竭力主张
definition_en: to strongly suggest that someone does something
example: The charity urged quick action.

definition_zh: n  顾问， 辅导顾问
definition_en: someone whose job is to help and support people with problems
example: Are you seeing a counsellor?

definition_zh: n  高科技
example: Comparing former films and modern high-tech films, which kind do you like better and why?

definition_zh: n  四周有篱笆的场地， 围场； 附件
definition_en: an area surrounded by a wall or fence, and used for a particular purpose
example: the bear enclosure at the zoo

definition_zh: adj  视觉的， 用于视觉的
definition_en: relating to seeing
example: Artists translate their ideas into visual images.

definition_zh: adj  不在场的； 心不在焉的
definition_en: not at work, school, a meeting etc, because you are sick or decide not to go
example: The dull, absent look on her face implied boredom.

definition_zh: n  帝国
definition_en: a group of countries that are all controlled by one ruler or government
example: the Roman empire

definition_zh: adj  体谅的； 宽容的; n 理解；谅解
definition_en: sympathetic and kind about other people’s problems; the ability to understand people’s behaviour and to forgive them when they do something wrong
example: Luckily, I have a very under­standing boss.

definition_zh: vt  利用， 运用
example: The one thing that never crosses the minds of our elite is to utilise local talent, local enterprise, local money to build Pakistan.

definition_zh: n  圣堂， 圣殿， 圣坛； 圣地； 庇护所， 避难所； 禁猎区， 动物保护区
definition_en: an area for birds or animals where they are protected and cannot be hunted
example: His church became a sanctuary for thousands of people who fled the civil war.

definition_zh: n  有氧运动
definition_en: a very active type of physical exercise done to music, usually in a class
example: Yoga has become the new aerobics and some people are getting it out of a book or video.

definition_zh: vt  教导； 指示， 命令
definition_en: to officially tell someone what to do
example: Eva went straight to the hotel, as instructed (= as she had been told ).

definition_zh: n 向上的斜坡；增加，改善; vt  使升级， 提高， 改进； 提拔， 提升； 改善
definition_en: to make a computer, machine, or piece of software better and able to do more things
example: The hotel has recently been refurbished and upgraded.

definition_zh: adj  有偏见的； 片面的
definition_en: unfairly preferring one person or group over another
example: Of course I’m biased, but I thought my daughter’s paintings were the best.

definition_zh: n  社会； 社区； 团体； 群落； 共同体
definition_en: the people who live in the same area, town etc
example: The new arts centre will serve the whole community.

definition_zh: n  疾病
definition_en: an illness that is not very serious
example: minor ailments

definition_zh: n  样式； 型号； 种类； 说法； 版本； 译本
definition_en: a copy of something that has been changed so that it is slightly different
example: the human version of mad cow disease

definition_zh: n  喷射剂； 推进物， 推进剂
definition_en: Propellant is a substance that causes something to move forward
example: ...a propellant for nuclear rockets.

definition_zh: adj 被抛弃的；衰退的，破败的; n  遗弃物； 无家可归者， 社会弃儿
definition_en: a derelict building or piece of land is in very bad condition because it has not been used for a long time; someone who has no money or home, lives on the streets, and is very dirty – used to show disapproval
example: Her body was found dumped in a derelict warehouse less than a mile from her home.

definition_zh: n  观念， 概念
definition_en: an idea about what something is like, or a general understanding of something
example: My conception of a garden was based on gardens I had visited in England.

definition_zh: n  青春， 青春期
definition_en: the time, usually between the ages of 12 and 18, when a young person is developing into an adult
example: Some young people suddenly become self-conscious and tongue-tied in early adolescence.

definition_zh: n 细节，详情；枝节，琐事; vt  详述， 详细说明
definition_en: a single feature, fact, or piece of information about something; to list things or give all the facts or information about something
example: This issue will be discussed in more detail in Chapter 5.

definition_zh: v  呼出； 散发
definition_en: to breathe air, smoke etc out of your mouth
example: Take a deep breath, then exhale slowly.

definition_zh: adj 适当的[ə'prəuprieɪt]; vt  占有， 挪用； 拨出
definition_en: correct or suitable for a particular time, situation, or purpose; to take something for yourself when you do not have the right to do this
example: The timing of the announcement was particularly appropriate .

definition_zh: v  谨防， 当心
definition_en: used to warn someone to be careful because something is dangerous
example: Police warned drivers to beware.

definition_zh: n 功用，效用；
definition_en: a piece of computer software that has a particular use
example: It’s a simple shareware utility that allows you to print signs and banners.

definition_zh: adv  立即， 马上； 紧接地； 直接地
definition_en: without delay
example: Mix in the remaining ingredients and serve immediately.

definition_zh: n  交响乐
definition_en: a long piece of music usually in four parts, written for an  orchestra
example: Bruckner’s Fifth Symphony

definition_zh: adj  乐观的， 乐观主义的
definition_en: believing that good things will happen in the future
example: Andrew took a more optimistic view.

definition_zh: adj  水底的， 海底的; n 潜水艇
definition_en: growing or used under the sea; a ship, especially a military one, that can stay under water
example: submarine plant life

definition_zh: adj 光滑而有光泽的；时髦的；豪华的; vt  使光滑发亮
definition_en: sleek hair or fur is straight, shiny, and healthy-looking; to make hair or fur smooth and shiny by putting water or oil on it
example: the sleek lines of the new Mercedes

definition_zh: adj  悲惨的； 灾难性的
definition_en: Something that is catastrophic involves or causes a sudden terrible disaster
example: A tidal wave caused by the earthquake hit the coast causing catastrophic damage.

definition_zh: n  极少量； 微粒
definition_en: a very small piece of something
example: dust particles

definition_zh: v  下来， 下降； 遗传
definition_en: to move from a higher level to a lower one
example: Our plane started to descend.

definition_zh: n 风味，滋味; vt  给…调味
definition_en: the particular taste of a food or drink; to give something a particular taste or more taste
example: Which flavour do you want - chocolate or vanilla?

definition_zh: n  密集； 浓度， 密度
definition_en: the degree to which an area is filled with people or things
example: areas of high population density

definition_zh: adj  荒谬的； 可笑的
definition_en: very silly or unreasonable
example: That’s a ridiculous idea!

definition_zh: n  闯入； 打搅； 侵扰
definition_en: when someone does something, or something happens, that affects your private life or activities in an unwanted way
example: the unwelcome intrusion of the press

definition_zh: adj  严重的； 严厉的； 剧烈的
definition_en: severe problems, injuries, illnesses etc are very bad or very serious
example: His injuries were quite severe.

definition_zh: n  路径， 途径
definition_en: A pathway is a path that you can walk along or a route that you can take
example: Richard was coming up the pathway.

definition_zh: n 板条，狭板; vt  用板条制作
definition_en: a thin flat piece of wood, plastic etc, used especially in furniture; to provide with slats
example: Oak clads the front walls, while slat covers a wall at the rear.

definition_zh: vi  出现； 显露， 暴露
definition_en: to appear or come out from somewhere
example: The flowers emerge in the spring.

definition_zh: vt  作为…的典型； 例证， 举例说明
definition_en: to be a very typical example of something
example: The building exemplifies the style of architecture which was popular at the time.

definition_zh: n 租约; vt  出租， 租用
definition_en: a legal agreement which allows you to use a building, car etc for a period of time, in return for rent; to use a building, car etc under a lease
example: The landlord refused to renew his lease.

definition_zh: vt  剥削； 开发， 开拓； 利用; n  英勇行为； 业绩， 功绩
definition_en: to treat someone unfairly by asking them to do things for you, but giving them very little in return – used to show disapproval; a brave and exciting adventure that someone has had
example: Homeworkers can easily be exploited by employers.

definition_zh: n  根据， 证据； 形迹， 迹象
definition_en: facts or signs that show clearly that something exists or is true
example: Murrow’s evidence was enough to convict Hayes of murder.

definition_zh: adj  致命的， 破坏性的， 毁灭性的； 有害的
definition_en: likely to be powerful or dangerous – often used humorously
example: They were all drinking lethal amounts of tequila!

definition_zh: v  跳跃； 涌现， 突然出现； 突然提出; n 春天，春季；弹簧，发条；弹性，弹力；泉；跳跃
definition_en: to suddenly become active, start moving, or start working; the season between winter and summer when leaves and flowers appear
example: They were prepared and ready to spring into action.

definition_zh: n  判定， 评定； 核定的付款额； 看法， 评价
definition_en: a process in which you make a judgment about a person or situation, or the judgment you make
example: a reading assessment test

definition_zh: n  自治， 自治权； 人身自由， 自主权
definition_en: freedom that a place or an organization has to govern or control itself
example: campaigners who want greater autonomy for Corsica

definition_zh: n  健康； 适合
definition_en: when you are healthy and strong enough to do hard work or play sports
example: an exercise programme to improve your fitness

definition_zh: adj 女的；
definition_en: relating to women or girls
example: female voters

definition_zh: adj 催眠的; n  催眠药
definition_en: making you feel tired or unable to pay attention to anything else, especially because of a regularly repeated sound or movement; a drug that helps you to sleep
example: His voice had a smooth hypnotic effect .

definition_zh: n  部长； 外交使节； 牧师
definition_en: a politician who is in charge of a government depart-ment, in Britain and some other countries
example: a senior Cabinet minister

definition_zh: adj  残忍的， 残暴的
definition_en: making someone suffer or feel unhappy
example: His death was a cruel blow .

definition_zh: n 阴凉处；罩；暗部；色度；细微差别; vt  遮蔽， 遮光； 把…涂暗
definition_en: slight darkness or shelter from the direct light of the sun made by something blocking it; to protect something from direct light
example: a plant that needs a lot of shade

definition_zh: v  聚集， 集合， 聚拢； 收集， 采集； 逐渐增加； 猜想， 推测
definition_en: to come together and form a group, or to make people do this
example: A crowd gathered to watch the fight.

definition_zh: v  坚持， 坚决认为； 一定要
definition_en: to say firmly and often that something is true, especially when other people think it may not be true
example: Stay for supper – I insist!

definition_zh: n  卷， 册； 体积； 音量
definition_en: the amount of sound produced by a television, radio etc
example: the volume of trade

definition_zh: vt  集中体现； 概括
n 指导方针，指导原则
definition_en: rules or instructions about the best way to do something
example: This chapter gives you some guidelines to help you in your work.

definition_zh: adj  亚热带的
definition_en: related to or typical of an area that is near a tropical area
example: subtropical vegetation

definition_zh: adj  松的； 散漫的； 不精确的
definition_en: not firmly fastened in place
example: a loose floorboard

definition_zh: n  喷出； 排出物
example: The amount or rate of emission or ejection.

definition_zh: n  火石， 打火石
definition_en: a type of smooth hard stone that makes a small flame when you hit it with steel
example: ...a flint arrowhead.

definition_zh: n  排成直线； 联盟
definition_en: the state of being arranged in a line with something or parallel to something
example: The church should have no political alignment.

definition_zh: adj  多刺的； 痛苦的， 棘手的
definition_en: a question etc that is complicated and difficult
example: the thorny question of immigration policy

definition_zh: adj  开发的， 利用的； 剥削的
definition_en: taking as much as possible from someone or something and giving very little in return
example: the exploitative nature of multinational companies

definition_zh: adj  私人的； 私下的； 私立的
definition_en: for use by one person or group, not for everyone
example: Morris has a private jet.

definition_zh: adj  技术的， 工艺的
definition_en: connected with knowledge of how machines work
example: Our staff will be available to give you technical support .

definition_zh: n  共事者； 同伴
definition_en: someone you spend a lot of time with, especially a friend
example: For ten years he had been her constant companion .

definition_zh: vi  告终； 结束
definition_en: If you say that an activity, process, or series of events culminates in or with a particular event, you mean that event happens at the end of it
example: They had an argument, which culminated in Tom getting drunk.

definition_zh: n  垮台， 倒塌， 破裂； 衰竭， 衰弱； 损坏， 故障； 分类
definition_en: an occasion when a car or a piece of machinery breaks and stops working
example: Always carry a phone with you in case you have a breakdown on the motorway.

definition_zh: n  不一致； 不和谐
definition_en: disagreement about important things which makes people be unfriendly to each other
example: ...racial disharmony.

definition_zh: v 伸展；延伸，延续，拉长；使倾注全力，使紧张; n  一段； 伸展， 延伸， 延续
definition_en: to straighten your arms, legs, or body to full length; A stretch of road, water, or land is a length or area of it
example: Carl sat up in bed, yawned, and stretched.

definition_zh: vt  达到， 完成， 实现
definition_en: to succeed in doing something, especially after trying very hard
example: We have accomplished all we set out to do.

definition_zh: n  股， 缕； 部分， 方面
definition_en: a single thin piece of thread, wire, hair etc
example: She tried to blow a grey strand of hair from her eyes.

definition_zh: adj  组成的； 有宪法制定或修改权的; n 成分，要素；选区内的选民
definition_en: being one of the parts of something; someone who votes in a particular area
example: the EU and its constituent members

definition_zh: vt  使烦躁； 使疼痛； 刺激
definition_en: to make someone feel annoyed or impatient, especially by doing something many times or for a long period of time
example: It really irritates me when he doesn’t help around the house.

definition_zh: adj  有责任感的； 可靠的
definition_en: if someone is responsible for an accident, mistake, crime etc, it is their fault or they can be blamed
example: We are determined to bring the people responsible to justice.

definition_zh: n  亚麻织品； 亚麻布
definition_en: cloth made from the flax plant, used to make high quality clothes, home decorations etc
example: a linen jacket

definition_zh: n  枪弹， 子弹
definition_en: a small piece of metal that you fire from a gun
example: He was killed by a single bullet.

definition_zh: vt  照亮； 阐明， 解释； 启发
definition_en: to make a light shine on something, or to fill a place with light
example: A single candle illuminated his face.

definition_zh: v  结束引语
v  伸出； 突出
definition_en: to stick out from somewhere
example: ...a huge round mass of smooth rock protruding from the water.

definition_zh: adj  滑的， 滑溜的； 油滑的， 狡猾的； 棘手的
definition_en: something that is slippery is difficult to hold, walk on etc because it is wet or  greasy
example: In places, the path can be wet and slippery.

definition_zh: n  重要性， 意义
definition_en: The significance of something is the importance that it has, usually because it will have an effect on a situation or shows something about a situation
example: Ideas about the social significance of religion have changed over time.

definition_zh: n  论坛； 讨论会
definition_en: an organization, meeting, TV programme etc where people have a chance to publicly discuss an important subject
example: Members of the council agreed that was an important forum for discussion.

definition_zh: n  轻视， 鄙视， 不尊重； 蔑视
definition_en: a feeling that someone or something is not important and deserves no respect
example: The public is treated with contempt by broadcasters.

definition_zh: n  地平线； 范围； 眼界
definition_en: the line far away where the land or sea seems to meet the sky
example: In the distance, the dot of a boat appeared on the horizon.

definition_zh: n  周围状况； 环境
definition_en: the air, water, and land on Earth, which is affected by man’s activities
example: Some of these chemicals are very damaging to the environment.

definition_zh: vt  出卖， 泄露； 辜负； 流露情感
definition_en: to show feelings that you are trying to hide
example: His voice betrayed his nervousness.

definition_zh: n  免费电话
definition_en: A Freefone telephone number is one which you can dial without having to pay for the call

definition_zh: adj 故意的；深思熟虑的；从容不迫的[dɪ'lIbəreIt]; v  深思熟虑； 审议
definition_en: intended or planned
example: a deliberate attempt to humiliate her

definition_zh: v  宣； 诅咒
definition_en: to use rude and offensive language
example: Don’t swear in front of the children.

definition_zh: n  轴， 柄， 杆； 矛柄， 把柄； 束， 光线； 竖井， 升降井
definition_en: a passage which goes down through a building or down into the ground, so that someone or something can get in or out
example: a drive shaft

definition_zh: n  大气； 气氛； 环境
definition_en: the feeling that an event or place gives you
example: The hotel had a lovely relaxed atmosphere .

definition_zh: v  装饰， 装潢， 布置； 授勋
definition_en: to paint the inside of a room, put special paper on the walls etc
example: The bathroom is decorated in green and yellow.

definition_zh: n  生涯； 经历； 职业
definition_en: a job or profession that you have been trained for, and which you do for a long period of your life
example: a teaching career

definition_zh: n  占领； 职业； 消遣
definition_en: a job or profession
example: Please state your name, address and occupation.

definition_zh: v  松弛， 放松； 放慢； 萧条
definition_en: to gradually become slower, weaker, less active etc, or to make something do this
example: The heavy rain showed no signs of slackening off.

definition_zh: vt  记住， 熟记
example: This involved being shown a picture of eight animals and being asked to memorise the order in which the creatures appeared.

definition_zh: vt 释放，解放；发表，发行; n  释放； 发表， 发布； 排放， 泄露
definition_en: to let someone go free, after having kept them somewhere; when someone is officially allowed to go free, after being kept somewhere
example: Police arrested several men, who were later released.

definition_zh: n 纲领，宣言；宪章；包租; vt  包租； 特许设立， 给…发许可证， 给予特权
definition_en: a statement of the principles, duties, and purposes of an organization; to pay a company for the use of their aircraft, boat etc
example: the freedoms embodied in the UN charter

definition_zh: vi  变苗条; adj 苗条的；薄的
definition_en: someone who is slim is attractively thin
example: How do you keep so slim?

definition_zh: vt  使洁净， 使纯洁， 净化； 提纯， 精炼
definition_en: to remove dirty or harmful substances from something
example: chemicals used to purify the water

definition_zh: adj 道德的，伦理的
definition_en: relating to the principles of what is right and wrong behaviour, and with the difference between good and evil
example: It is easy to have an opinion on a moral issue like the death penalty for murder.

definition_zh: adj  精神病的； 治疗精神病的
definition_en: relating to the study and treatment of mental illness
example: a psychiatric hospital
